Corollary 592

[Flattening stratification for a coherent sheaf on a relative curve] Let \(k\) be a field, \(C\) a smooth proper curve over \(k\), and \(T\) a noetherian \(k\)-scheme. Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a coherent sheaf on \(C \times _k T\). Then the flattening-stratification conclusion of 583 applies: there is a finite set \(I\) of Hilbert polynomials and locally-closed subschemes \(T_f \hookrightarrow T\) for \(f \in I\), set-theoretically covering \(T\) disjointly, such that (a) \(\mathcal{F}|_{C \times _k T_f}\) is flat over \(T_f\) for each \(f\), and (b) any morphism \(\varphi : T' \to T\) such that \(\varphi ^* \mathcal{F}\) on \(C \times _k T'\) is \(T'\)-flat factors uniquely through some \(T_f\).

LaTeX Lean
Corollary 577

[Locally trivial \(\Rightarrow \) finite type] Under the hypotheses of 576, the locally trivial module \(M\) is of finite type (\(M.\mathtt{IsFiniteType}\)). Quasi-coherence (\(M.\mathtt{IsQuasicoherent}\)) follows from finite presentation via \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.instIsQuasicoherentOfIsFinitePresentation}\).

LaTeX Lean
Corollary 909

[A genus-\(0\) curve admits a non-constant rational function with a single simple pole]

Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, Exercise 1.1, p. 297. Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over \(\bar k\) with \(g(C) = 0\), and let \(P \in C\) be a closed point. Then there exists a non-constant rational function \(f \in K(C)\) such that:

  • \(f\) is regular on \(C \setminus \{ P\} \), i.e. \(\operatorname {ord}_Q(f) \geq 0\) for every closed point \(Q \in C\) with \(Q \neq P\);

  • \(f\) has at most a simple pole at \(P\), i.e. \(\operatorname {ord}_P(f) \geq -1\);

  • \(f \notin \bar k\), equivalently \(\div (f) \neq 0 \in \mathrm{Div}(C)\).

In particular, the pair \((1, f)\) spans \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))\) as a \(\bar k\)-vector space.

LaTeX Lean
Corollary 767

[Regular local rings are Cohen–Macaulay] Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00OD (lemma-regular-ring-CM). Every regular Noetherian local ring is Cohen–Macaulay: if \((R, \mathfrak {m})\) is regular local of Krull dimension \(d\), then \(\text{depth}(R) = d = \dim (R)\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 102Universe-bump linear equivalence

Let \(R\) be a ring and \(C\) an \(R\)-linear abelian category. For any two universes at which \(\operatorname{Ext}\) exists, there is an \(R\)-linear isomorphism between the two \(\operatorname{Ext}\) groups, obtained by upgrading the universe-change bijection with its additivity and \(R\)-linearity.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 48

[Linear hom-equivalence from an adjunction]

Let \(F \dashv G\) be an adjunction between \(R\)-linear preadditive categories with \(G\) additive and \(R\)-linear. Then for all objects \(X\) and \(Y\), the additive equivalence of the adjunction upgrades to an \(R\)-linear equivalence between \(\operatorname{Hom}(FX, Y)\) and \(\operatorname{Hom}(X, GY)\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 236\(\bar k\)-algebra structure on the homogeneous away-localization

For a polynomial \(f \in \bar k[X_0, X_1]\), the \(\bar k\)-algebra structure on the homogeneous localization \(\mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, f\) obtained by composing the algebra map \(\bar k \to \mathcal A_0\) into the degree-\(0\) piece with the canonical map \(\mathcal A_0 \to \mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, f\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 881Constant-to-skyscraper presheaf map on \(\mathrm{PUnit}\)

On the one-point space \(\mathrm{PUnit}\), the presheaf morphism from the constant presheaf with value \(A\) to the skyscraper presheaf at \(\mathrm{PUnit}.\mathrm{unit}\) with value \(A\): the identity (via \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\)) on the non-empty open \(\top \), and the terminal map on the empty open \(\bot \). It is the forward leg of the inner iso of 867.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 882Skyscraper-to-constant presheaf map on \(\mathrm{PUnit}\)

On the one-point space \(\mathrm{PUnit}\), the presheaf morphism from the skyscraper presheaf at \(\mathrm{PUnit}.\mathrm{unit}\) with value \(A\) to the underlying presheaf of the constant sheaf with value \(A\): the identity composed with the sheafification unit on \(\top \), and the terminal map on \(\bot \). It is the inverse leg, modulo sheafification, of 867.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 147

[Čech complex of a quasi-coherent sheaf] Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial (standard-cover Čech vanishing). Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup _{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with all intersections \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) affine (e.g. \(X\) separated). The Čech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet (\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) is the cochain complex obtained by applying the global-sections functor to the Čech nerve of Definition 146 evaluated at \(\mathcal{F}\); in degree \(p\) it is

\[ \check{\mathcal{C}}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = \prod _{(i_0, \ldots , i_p) \in I^{p+1}} \mathcal{F}\bigl(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr), \]

with differential \(d : \check{\mathcal{C}}^p \to \check{\mathcal{C}}^{p+1}\) the alternating sum \((d s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} = \sum _{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\, s_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}}\) of the restriction maps. Over an affine \(U = \operatorname {Spec}(A)\) with \(\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}\) and a standard cover by the \(D(f_i)\), this is the complex \(\prod _{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod _{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \to \cdots \) of localisations. The relative Čech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet (\mathfrak {U}, \mathcal{F})\) for \(f : X \to S\) is the analogous complex in \(\operatorname {QCoh}(S)\) obtained by applying \(f_*\) over each finite intersection instead of global sections; its terms are \(\prod _{i_0 \ldots i_p} (f|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})_* \bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr)\), and it is a complex in \(\operatorname {QCoh}(S)\) because each \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine and the pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated morphism is quasi-coherent.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 158

[Unconditional higher direct image via Čech] Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application; unconditional Čech packaging is Archon-original. Let \(f : X \to S\) be a separated quasi-compact morphism and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Fix a finite affine open cover \(\mathfrak {U}\) of \(X\). The (unconditional) \(i\)-th higher direct image is defined, for each \(i \geq 0\), as the \(i\)-th cohomology sheaf of the relative Čech complex,

\[ R^i f_*\mathcal{F} \; :=\; H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet (\mathfrak {U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr) \in \operatorname {QCoh}(S). \]

This requires no hypothesis that the category of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules have enough injectives: the right-hand side is the cohomology of an explicit complex of quasi-coherent sheaves. By Lemma 157 the result is canonically isomorphic to the derived-functor higher direct image wherever the latter is defined, and in particular is independent of the chosen affine cover \(\mathfrak {U}\) up to canonical isomorphism: two affine covers admit a common affine refinement, and the induced refinement maps on Čech complexes are quasi-isomorphisms because both compute the same \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) by Lemma 157. For \(i = 0\) one recovers the ordinary pushforward \(R^0 f_*\mathcal{F} = f_*\mathcal{F}\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 146

[Čech nerve of an affine open cover] Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §Čech cohomology of quasi-coherent sheaves. Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup _{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite open cover of \(X\) by affine opens, with index set \(I\). For a finite nonempty tuple \(i_0, \ldots , i_p \in I\) write \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = U_{i_0} \cap \cdots \cap U_{i_p}\) for the corresponding intersection. The Čech nerve of \(\mathcal{U}\) is the augmented cosimplicial object in \(\operatorname {QCoh}(X)\) whose object in cosimplicial degree \(p\) is the direct image, along the open immersions \(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p} : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\), of the restriction of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) (or of any chosen quasi-coherent sheaf) to the \((p+1)\)-fold intersections,

\[ [p] \; \longmapsto \; \prod _{(i_0, \ldots , i_p) \in I^{p+1}} (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_* \, \bigl(\, \cdot \, |_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr), \]

with cofaces given by the restriction maps that omit one index and codegeneracies by the maps that repeat one index; the augmentation in degree \(-1\) is the object on all of \(X\). The variance is essential: the geometric nerve of the cover (the iterated fibre powers of \(\coprod _i U_i\) over \(X\), introduced in §7.2.1 below) is a simplicial object in schemes, but applying the covariant direct-image functor turns it into a cosimplicial object in \(\operatorname {QCoh}(X)\); consequently the associated alternating-coface complex is a cochain complex (differentials raising degree), not a chain complex. When \(X\) is separated each intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is itself affine. The local affine model is the standard open covering \(U = \bigcup _{i=1}^n D(f_i)\) of an affine open \(U = \operatorname {Spec}(A)\) by basic opens, where \(f_1, \ldots , f_n \in A\) generate the unit ideal; on such a cover the intersections are again basic opens \(D(f_{i_0} \cdots f_{i_p})\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 249The chart-\(i\) evaluation ring map

The ring map \(\bar k[X_0, X_1] \to \bar k[u]\) (with \(\bar k[u] = \mathrm{MvPolynomial}\, \mathrm{Unit}\, \bar k\)) sending \(X_i \mapsto 1\) and \(X_{\mathrm{other}\, i} \mapsto u\) (and constants to constants). This dehomogenisation underlies the forward chart-ring map (253).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 790

[Codim-\(1\) cycle group / Weil divisor group of a scheme]

Source: Hartshorne, II.6, p. 130 (Weil divisor group). Let \(X\) be a Noetherian integral separated scheme that is regular in codimension one (i.e. \(X\) satisfies \((*)\)). The Weil divisor group

\[ \mathrm{Div}(X) \; =\; \bigoplus _{\substack {Y \subset X \\ \text{prime divisor}}} \mathbb Z \cdot Y \]

is the free abelian group on the set of prime divisors of \(X\) (790); an element \(D = \sum n_i Y_i\) with finitely many nonzero coefficients \(n_i \in \mathbb Z\) is called a Weil divisor, and is effective when all \(n_i \geq 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 674

[Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps] Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16 (the conclusion). Let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map between varieties over \(\bar k\). We say \(f\) is codim-1-indeterminacy-free (or has codimension-\(\geq 2\) indeterminacy) if every irreducible component of the closed subset \(Z(f) \subseteq X\) has codimension \(\geq 2\) in \(X\). Equivalently, no prime divisor of \(X\) is contained in \(Z(f)\); equivalently, every point \(\eta \in X\) with \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{ \eta \} } = 1\) lies in \(\mathrm{Dom}(f)\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 766

[Cohen–Macaulay local ring] Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00N4 (definition-local-ring-CM). A Noetherian local ring \((R, \mathfrak {m})\) is called Cohen–Macaulay if \(\text{depth}(R) = \dim (R)\) (equivalently, if \(R\) is Cohen–Macaulay as a module over itself, where Cohen–Macaulay for a finite \(R\)-module \(M\) means \(\dim (\text{Supp}(M)) = \text{depth}(M)\), cf. Stacks tag 00N3).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 579

[Coherent sheaf flat over the base] Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(f : X \to S\) a morphism of finite type, and \(\mathcal{F}\) a coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. We say \(\mathcal{F}\) is flat over \(S\) (equivalently, \(\mathcal{F}\) is \(f\)-flat, or \(S\)-flat) if for every point \(x \in X\) the stalk \(\mathcal{F}_x\) is a flat \(\mathcal{O}_{S, f(x)}\)-module, where the \(\mathcal{O}_{S, f(x)}\)-module structure is given by the composite \(\mathcal{O}_{S, f(x)} \to \mathcal{O}_{X, x} \to \operatorname{End}(\mathcal{F}_x)\). Source: cf. [Stacks Project], tag 00HB (flat modules); [Nitsure], §4 passim. Equivalently (after pulling back to an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}A \subseteq S\) and an affine open \(V = \operatorname{Spec}B \subseteq f^{-1}(U)\) with \(\mathcal{F}|_V = \widetilde{M}\) for \(M\) a finite \(B\)-module), \(\mathcal{F}|_V\) is \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-flat in the sense that \(M\) is a flat \(A\)-module (with the \(A\)-module structure coming from the ring map \(A \to B\)).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 148

[Arrow of an affine cover] Let \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup _{i \in I} U_i\) be an affine open cover. Its cover arrow is the single morphism \(\coprod _{i \in I} U_i \longrightarrow X\) obtained as the universal map out of the coproduct induced by the open immersions \(U_i \hookrightarrow X\). Packaging the cover as one arrow lets the generic Čech-nerve-of-an-arrow machinery apply.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 149

[Augmented Čech nerve of a cover] The augmented Čech nerve of the cover arrow of Definition 148: the augmented simplicial scheme over \(X\) whose degree-\(p\) object is the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power

\[ \Bigl(\coprod _{i} U_i\Bigr)^{\times _X (p+1)} = \coprod _{(i_0, \ldots , i_p) \in I^{p+1}} U_{i_0} \times _X \cdots \times _X U_{i_p}, \]

with augmentation the cover map to \(X\). It exists unconditionally because the category of schemes has all finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks underlying the Čech nerve of an arrow). This is the geometric backbone (1).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 723

[Depth of a module] Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00LF (definition-depth). Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, let \(I \subseteq R\) be an ideal, and let \(M\) be a finitely generated \(R\)-module. The \(I\)-depth of \(M\), written \(\text{depth}_I(M)\), is the supremum in \(\{ 0, 1, 2, \dots , \infty \} \) of the lengths of \(M\)-regular sequences contained in \(I\), provided \(IM \neq M\); if \(IM = M\) we set \(\text{depth}_I(M) = \infty \). When \((R, \mathfrak {m})\) is a Noetherian local ring we abbreviate \(\text{depth}(M) := \text{depth}_{\mathfrak {m}}(M)\) and call this simply the depth of \(M\). The depth of the ring \(R\) itself is \(\text{depth}(R)\), the depth of \(R\) as a module over itself.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 633The relative-divisor functor as a carrier extraction

The presheaf \(\mathrm{Div}_{C/k} : (\operatorname{Sch}/k)^{op} \to \mathrm{Set}\) is the object whose existence is asserted by 632, extracted as a chosen witness. It is the open subfunctor of the Hilbert scheme cut out by the effective-divisor criterion of 624; once 24 lands, the extraction collapses to a re-export.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 818

[Divisor of a closed point on a curve]

Source: Hartshorne, II.6, p. 137 (“Divisors on Curves”). Let \(C\) be a smooth proper curve over a field \(k\) and let \(P \in C\) be a closed point. The Weil divisor associated to \(P\) is the element

\[ [P] \; :=\; 1 \cdot P \; \in \; \mathrm{Div}(C) \]

i.e. the prime divisor \(P\) with multiplicity one. The assignment is well-defined because the closed point \(P\) is automatically a codimension-\(1\) integral closed subscheme of the \(1\)-dimensional integral scheme \(C\). Conversely, every prime divisor on \(C\) is of this form, so an arbitrary divisor on \(C\) is a finite formal \(\mathbb Z\)-linear combination \(\sum n_i [P_i]\) of closed points.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 821

[Degree of a divisor on a curve over \(\bar k\)]

Source: Hartshorne, II.6, p. 137 (definition of degree on a nonsingular curve). Let \(C\) be a smooth proper curve over an algebraically closed field \(\bar k\). The degree of a Weil divisor \(D = \sum n_i [P_i] \in \mathrm{Div}(C)\) is the integer

\[ \deg (D) \; :=\; \sum _i n_i \; \in \; \mathbb Z. \]

The definition uses that every closed point of \(C\) has residue field \(\bar k\) (so each prime divisor contributes degree one); over a general field \(k\) the same definition with the residue-field-degree weight \(\deg (D) := \sum _i n_i \, [\kappa (P_i) : k]\) recovers the geometric degree, but the project’s RR bridge needs only the \(\bar k\) specialisation.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 663Degree of a Picard class

Source: [Milne], “Abelian Varieties” (course notes, 2008), §III.1, p. 88. Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of genus \(g\), and let \(\operatorname{Pic}_{C/k}\) be the Picard scheme of 628. The degree map

\[ \deg \; \colon \; \operatorname{Pic}_{C/k}(k) \longrightarrow \mathbb {Z} \]

is defined as follows: a \(k\)-point \(\lambda \in \operatorname{Pic}_{C/k}(k)\) represents (after étale base change) an invertible sheaf \(\mathcal{L}\) on \(C\); its Hilbert polynomial with respect to a fixed degree-one polarisation \(\mathcal{O}_C(1)\) on \(C\) is, by 593 and Riemann–Roch,

\[ \Phi _{\mathcal{L}}(n) \; =\; \chi (C, \mathcal{L} \otimes \mathcal{O}_C(n)) \; =\; n \cdot \deg \mathcal{L} + 1 - g, \]

a polynomial in \(n\) linear in \(n\). The integer \(\deg (\lambda ) := \deg \mathcal{L}\) is the leading coefficient of this polynomial, well-defined on the isomorphism class \([\mathcal{L}]\) and (because \(\operatorname{Pic}_{C/k}\) represents the étale-sheafified relative Picard functor) on the \(k\)-point \(\lambda \). The degree map is a group homomorphism: for \(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{M}\) invertible sheaves on \(C\) one has \(\deg (\mathcal{L} \otimes \mathcal{M}) = \deg \mathcal{L} + \deg \mathcal{M}\), because the Euler characteristic is additive on the additive groups of twists. Equivalently, the degree on \(k\)-points is the index of the connected component of \(\operatorname{Pic}_{C/k}\) containing \(\lambda \) under the Hilbert-polynomial decomposition of 625: the disjoint union \(\operatorname{Pic}_{C/k} = \coprod _{d \in \mathbb {Z}} \operatorname{Pic}^d_{C/k}\) is indexed by the degree, with \(\operatorname{Pic}^d_{C/k}\) the component representing classes of degree \(d\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 749Elementary linear map

For a commutative ring \(R\) and indices \(i \in \{ 1, \dots , n\} \), \(j \in \{ 1, \dots , m\} \), the elementary map \(E_{i,j} : R^m \to R^n\) is the \(R\)-linear map sending the standard basis vector \(\text{Pi.single}\, j\, 1\) to \(\text{Pi.single}\, i\, 1\) and every other standard basis vector to \(0\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 845

[Euler characteristic of a coherent sheaf on a curve]

Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, p. 295, the formula displayed inside the proof of Theorem 1.3. Let \(C\) be a smooth proper curve over \(\bar k\) and let \(\mathcal F\) be a coherent sheaf on \(C\). The Euler characteristic of \(\mathcal F\) is the integer

\[ \chi (\mathcal F) \; :=\; \sum _{i \geq 0} (-1)^i \, \dim _{\bar k} H^i(C, \mathcal F) \; =\; \dim _{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal F) \, -\, \dim _{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal F). \]

The second equality is a curve-specific simplification: because \(\dim C = 1\), Grothendieck vanishing (Hartshorne III.2.7) gives \(H^i(C, \mathcal F) = 0\) for \(i \geq 2\), so the alternating sum has only two terms. Coherence of \(\mathcal F\) on the proper \(\bar k\)-scheme \(C\) guarantees that both \(H^0\) and \(H^1\) are finite-dimensional \(\bar k\)-vector spaces (Serre’s coherent-cohomology finiteness theorem, the same finiteness backing 846), so the two terms are honest natural numbers and the difference is a well-defined integer.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 531Evaluation functional of a dual section

At an object \(X\), the \(R(X)\)-linear map \(M(X) \to R(X)\) obtained by evaluating a dual section \(\varphi \) at its terminal component.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 806

[Function-field isomorphism along an open immersion] Source: Archon-original packaging; built on Mathlib’s AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Opens.stalkIso + genericPoint_eq_of_isOpenImmersion. For an integral scheme \(X\) and a non-empty open subscheme \(U \hookrightarrow X\) with \(\texttt{IsIntegral}\, U.\texttt{toScheme}\), the function field of \(U.\texttt{toScheme}\) is canonically isomorphic to the function field of \(X\). The isomorphism is built by composing \(\texttt{Scheme.Opens.stalkIso}\, U\, (\texttt{genericPoint}\, U.\texttt{toScheme})\) with \(X.\texttt{presheaf.stalkCongr}\) along the equality \((\texttt{genericPoint}\, U.\texttt{toScheme}).\texttt{val} = \texttt{genericPoint}\, X\) coming from \(\texttt{genericPoint\_ eq\_ of\_ isOpenImmersion}\, U.\iota \). This is the entry point Sub-build 3 (iter-202) uses to discharge the \(\texttt{h\_ compat}\) hypothesis of \(\texttt{Scheme.PrimeDivisor.ordFrac\_ stalkIso\_ naturality}\) (iter-201, private), promoting the parameterised Sub-build 2 naturality to the canonical function-field iso of integral schemes.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 192\(\mathbb G_a\) as an object of \(\mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}\bar k)\)

The additive group object \(\mathbb G_a = \mathbb A^1_{\bar k}\) described in 191, packaged as an object of \(\mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}\bar k)\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 262\(\mathbb G_a\) as a bare scheme

The underlying scheme of the additive group \(\mathbb G_a\) over \(\operatorname{Spec}\bar k\): the affine line \(\mathbb A^1 = \mathtt{AffineSpace}\, (\mathrm{Fin}\, 1)\, (\operatorname{Spec}\bar k)\), whose global sections are \(\bar k[\mathrm{Fin}\, 1]\). The object \(\mathbb G_a\) (192) is this scheme with its structure morphism.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 215

[The \(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling action \(\sigma _\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1\) (primary), and the \(\mathbb G_a\)-translation companion \(\sigma \)]

The scaling action (primary). The multiplicative scaling action

\[ \sigma _\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1, \qquad \text{``}\sigma _\times (x, \lambda ) = \lambda x\text{''} \]

(ame gmScalingP1) is a genuine total scheme morphism on all of \(\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m\) — no indeterminacy, characteristic-free (Möbius scaling fixing both \(0\) and \(\infty \)) — defined chartwise:

  • On \(\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_m\) (target chart \(x\)): \((x, \lambda ) \mapsto \lambda x\), a polynomial map, regular everywhere.

  • Near \(\infty \) (target chart \(u = 1/x\)): the target coordinate \(1/(\lambda x)\) rewrites as \(u/\lambda \), regular everywhere on this chart because \(\lambda \in \mathbb G_m\) is invertible.

The two target charts cover the image and overlap consistently, so \(\sigma _\times \) is defined on the whole surface. It is a left action of the group \(\mathbb G_m\) on \(\mathbb P^1\): \(\sigma _\times (x, 1) = x\) and \(\sigma _\times (\sigma _\times (x, \lambda ), \mu ) = \sigma _\times (x, \lambda \mu )\). Crucially it has two fixed points, \(\sigma _\times (0, \lambda ) = 0\) and \(\sigma _\times (\infty , \lambda ) = \infty \) for all \(\lambda \); the fixed point \(0\) is what makes the \(W\)-axis of the additive decomposition collapse in the primary base-case proof (231, 229). Companion: the \(\mathbb G_a\)-translation action (demoted route only). For the Milne-faithful additive alternative we also record the translation action

\[ \sigma \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a \to \mathbb P^1, \qquad \text{``}\sigma (x, y) = x + y\text{''} \]

(ame gaTranslationP1 [expected]), likewise a total scheme morphism on all of \(\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a\), defined chartwise: on \(\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_a\) (chart \(x\)) it is the polynomial map \((x, y) \mapsto x + y\); near \(\infty \) (\(u = 1/x\)) the coordinate \(1/(x + y)\) rewrites as \(u/(1 + u y)\), regular at \(u = 0\) (value \(0\) there — \(\sigma \) fixes \(\infty \) for every \(y\)), while the locus \(x + y = 0\) is covered by the chart \(x\) on which it is the finite value \(0\). It is a left action of \(\mathbb G_a\) on \(\mathbb P^1\) fixing \(\infty \): \(\sigma (x, 0) = x\) and \(\sigma (\sigma (x, y), y') = \sigma (x, y + y')\). This companion underlies only the alternative proof of 228 and the demoted \(\mathbb G_a\)-additive close of the base case (230); it is not on the genus-\(0\) critical path.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 171

[Genus of a smooth proper curve]

The genus of a smooth proper curve \(C\) over \(k\) is the natural number

\[ g(C) \; =\; \dim _k H^1(C,\, \mathcal O_C), \]

the \(k\)-dimension of the first sheaf-cohomology group of the structure sheaf of \(C\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 191Concrete genus-\(0\) base objects: \(\mathbb P^1\), \(\mathbb G_a\), \(\mathbb G_m\) over \(\bar k\)

Work over \(\operatorname{Spec}\bar k\) with \(\bar k\) algebraically closed. The primary \(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling route (and its \(\mathbb G_a\)-additive alternative) needs only three concrete objects of \(\mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}\bar k)\):

  • \(\mathbb P^1 = \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\), the projective line — a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve of genus \(0\) (Mathlib: projective space, or \(\mathrm{Proj}\) of the graded polynomial ring; ame ProjectiveLineBar). It carries the distinguished \(\bar k\)-points \(0\), \(1\), \(\infty \) and the two standard affine charts \(\mathbb A^1 = \mathbb P^1 \setminus \{ \infty \} \) (coordinate \(x\)) and \(\mathbb P^1 \setminus \{ 0\} \) (coordinate \(u = 1/x\)).

  • \(\mathbb G_a = \mathbb A^1_{\bar k}\), the affine line as the additive group object (Mathlib: AffineSpace \(\mathbb A(1; \operatorname{Spec}\bar k)\) with the \(\mathtt{GrpObj}\) additive structure \((x, y) \mapsto x + y\), identity \(0\); ame Ga). As a scheme \(\mathbb G_a = \mathbb A^1 = \mathbb P^1 \setminus \{ \infty \} \), and the open immersion \(\mathbb G_a \hookrightarrow \mathbb P^1\) has dense (cofinite) image.

  • \(\mathbb G_m = \mathbb A^1 \setminus \{ 0\} = \mathbb P^1 \setminus \{ 0, \infty \} \), the multiplicative group object (group law \((x, y) \mapsto xy\), identity \(1\); ame Gm). Its open immersion into \(\mathbb P^1\) also has dense image.

All three are of finite type over \(\bar k\); \(\mathbb P^1\) is proper, while \(\mathbb G_a\) and \(\mathbb G_m\) are affine (hence not complete). These are the only “genus-\(0\)” geometric inputs the base case needs.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 322

[Genus-\(0\) Albanese witness]

Let \(C \in \mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}k)\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over \(k\) with \(\mathrm{genus}(C) = 0\). Then there exists a \(\mathtt{JacobianWitness}\, C\) whose underlying scheme is \(\operatorname{Spec}k\) (the terminal object of \(\mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}k)\)), with the trivial group-object structure, smoothness of relative dimension \(0\), properness, and geometric irreducibility, and whose \(\mathtt{isAlbaneseFor}\) field encodes the Albanese universal property for every \(k\)-rational marked point \(P : \mathbf{1} \to C\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 514Multiplication by a global scalar as an endomorphism

For \(f \in R(T)\), the endomorphism \(m_f^{N} : N \to N\) of a presheaf of modules \(N\) that at each object \(Y\) is multiplication by \(\mathtt{termRingMap}_Y\, f \in R(Y)\); its naturality is the heart of the global-scalar action on morphism modules.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 193\(\mathbb G_m\) as an object of \(\mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}\bar k)\)

The multiplicative group object \(\mathbb G_m = \mathbb A^1 \setminus \{ 0\} \) described in 191, packaged as an object of \(\mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}\bar k)\). In Lean this is encoded as the affine scheme \(\operatorname{Spec}(\mathrm{Localization.Away}\, t)\) (the spectrum of the localisation \(\bar k[t, t^{-1}]\)), not as the basic open subscheme \(D(t) \subset \mathbb A^1\); the two are canonically isomorphic but downstream consumers (the scaling action of 215 and the additivity-over-product input \(W = \mathbb G_m\) in 188) require an affine carrier.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 202The multiplicative \(\mathtt{GrpObj}\) structure on \(\mathbb G_m\)

The multiplicative group-object structure \((\mathbb G_m, \cdot , 1)\) in \(\mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}\bar k)\), recording the multiplication \((x, y) \mapsto xy\) and unit \(1\) of 193 as a Lean \(\mathtt{GrpObj}\) instance. This structure is consumed only as a carrier for the scaling action \(\sigma _\times \) of 215; the primary \(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling proof of 231 uses \(\mathbb G_m\) as a parameter scheme (via \(W\) in the additive decomposition), not its group-object axioms.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 197The multiplicative identity \(1 \in \mathbb G_m\)

The multiplicative identity \(1 \in \mathbb G_m(\bar k)\), supplied in Lean as the unit map \(\eta [\mathbb G_m] \colon \operatorname{Spec}\bar k \to \mathbb G_m\) in \(\mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}\bar k)\). It serves as the \(W\)-base point for the additive decomposition applied in the primary \(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling proof.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 278Backward of the representability bijection

Given a unit \(u \in \Gamma (T_{\mathrm{left}}, \top )^\times \), the morphism \(T \to \mathbb G_m\) determined by the ring map \(\bar k[t, t^{-1}] \to \Gamma (T_{\mathrm{left}}, \top )\) sending \(t \mapsto u\) (lifted through the localization since \(u\) is a unit), bundled with the over-\(\operatorname{Spec}\bar k\) commutativity 277.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 276Forward of the representability bijection

Given \(f \colon T \to \mathbb G_m\), the unit of \(\Gamma (T_{\mathrm{left}}, \top )^\times \) obtained as the image of the standard generator \(t \in \bar k[t, t^{-1}]\) (a unit) under the induced ring map \(\bar k[t, t^{-1}] \to \Gamma (T_{\mathrm{left}}, \top )\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 275Units-of-global-sections functor

The functor \((\mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}\bar k))^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathbf{Grp}\) sending \(T \mapsto \Gamma (T_{\mathrm{left}}, \top )^\times \), with functorial action by \(\mathtt{Units.map}\) of the pullback on global sections. This is the presheaf of groups that \(\mathbb G_m\) represents (282).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 282\(\mathbb G_m\) represents the units functor

The \(\mathtt{RepresentableBy}\) witness exhibiting \(\mathbb G_m\) as a representing object for the units-of-global-sections functor 275: the natural bijection \(\mathrm{Hom}(-, \mathbb G_m) \cong \Gamma (-, \top )^\times \) with components 276/278, round-trips 279/280, and naturality 281. This is the witness feeding the \(\mathtt{GrpObj}\, \mathbb G_m\) structure 202.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 267The ring \(\bar k[t, t^{-1}]\)

The ring \(\bar k[t, t^{-1}] = \mathtt{Localization.Away}\, t\), localization of \(\bar k[t] = \mathrm{MvPolynomial}\, \mathrm{Unit}\, \bar k\) at the powers of \(t = X_{()}\). This is the global-sections ring of \(\mathbb G_m\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 219

[The chart-\(i\) scheme morphism for \(\sigma _\times \)]

For each chart index \(i \in \{ 0, 1\} = \mathrm{Fin}\, 2\), the chart-\(i\) scheme morphism

\[ \mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ chart}\, \bar k\, i \colon (\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover}\, \bar k).X\, i \longrightarrow \mathtt{ProjectiveLineBarScheme}\, \bar k \]

realising \(\sigma _\times = \mathtt{gmScalingP1}\) (215) on the \(i\)-th chart of the pullback cover \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover}\) (218). On chart \(1\) (target affine chart \(D_+(X_1)\), coordinate \(u = X_0/X_1\)) the morphism realises \((x, \lambda ) \mapsto \lambda x\) as the ring-level map \(u \mapsto u \otimes \lambda \) in \(\mathrm{Localization.Away}\, t \otimes _{\bar k} \mathtt{GmRing}\, \bar k\) (here \(t\) is the \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\, \{ *\} \, \bar k\) generator); on chart \(0\) (target affine chart \(D_+(X_0)\), coordinate \(t = X_1/X_0\)) it realises \(1/(\lambda x) = u/\lambda \) as the ring-level map \(t \mapsto t \otimes \lambda ^{-1}\). The two chart-side ring maps are packaged in Lean as \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ chart0\_ ringMap}\) and \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ chart1\_ ringMap}\) (both axiom-clean iter-171). Construction recipe. The scheme morphism is assembled from the chart-side ring map by the standard “ring map \(\Rightarrow \) scheme map between \(\operatorname{Spec}\)’s of relative tensor products” bridge, applied to the affine carrier of each cover chart. Concretely the chain is \(\mathtt{pullbackSpecIso}\) (identifying \((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover})_i\) with \(\operatorname{Spec}\) of an \(\bar k\)-tensor product of the chart-\(i\) chart ring with \(\mathtt{GmRing}\)), followed by \(\operatorname{Spec}.\mathrm{map}\) of the ring map above, followed by the chart-ring iso of 204 (which identifies \(\mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, X_i\) with the affine chart ring \(\bar k[u]\) at the \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\, \{ *\} \, \bar k\)-level), and then \(\mathrm{Proj.away}\, \iota \) to land in \(\mathbb P^1.\mathrm{left}\). The substantive step that is not algebraic (the chart-ring iso 204 is already axiom-clean via the iter-172 206) is the source-side \(\mathtt{pullbackSpecIso}\) identification, which navigates the abstract \(\mathtt{pullback}\, (\mathtt{pullback.fst}\, \mathbb P^1.\mathrm{hom}\, \mathbb G_m.\mathrm{hom})\, (\mathrm{Proj.away}\, \iota \, \mathcal A\, X_i)\) into the \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-of-tensor-product form. The full recipe is recorded in the iter-173 analogist note \(\mathtt{analogies/chart{\text-}bridge.md}\) (in flight), and the prior 6-step decomposition in \(\mathtt{analogies/gmscaling{\text-}deep.md}\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 286Chart-0 ring map of \(\sigma _\times \)

At the \(\bar k[u]\)-level, the ring map sending the affine coordinate \(t = X_1/X_0\) to \(t \otimes \lambda ^{-1}\), where \(\lambda ^{-1} = \mathtt{invSelf}\, t \in \bar k[t, t^{-1}]\) — the local form of the scaling near \(\infty \), regular because \(\lambda \) is invertible.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 285Chart-1 ring map of \(\sigma _\times \)

At the \(\bar k[u]\)-level, the ring map sending the affine coordinate \(u = X_0/X_1\) to \(u \otimes \lambda \), where \(\lambda = t \in \bar k[t, t^{-1}]\) — the local form of the scaling \((x, \lambda ) \mapsto \lambda x\) near \(0\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 218

[Pullback open cover of \(\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m\) along the chart cover of \(\mathbb P^1\)]

The two-chart affine open cover of \((\mathbb P^1 \otimes \mathbb G_m).\mathrm{left}\) obtained by pulling back the chart cover of 203 along the first pullback projection \(\mathrm{pr}_1 \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1\) (Mathlib’s \(\mathtt{pullback.fst}\, \mathbb P^1.\mathrm{hom}\, \mathbb G_m.\mathrm{hom}\)). Concretely the \(i\)-th chart is

\[ (\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover})_i = \mathrm{pullback}\bigl(\mathtt{pullback.fst}\, \mathbb P^1.\mathrm{hom}\, \mathbb G_m.\mathrm{hom}, \mathrm{Proj.away}\, \iota \, \mathcal A\, X_i\bigr), \]

i.e. the affine open \(D_+(X_i) \times \mathbb G_m \subseteq \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m\). This cover is the \(\mathtt{Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms}\) source on which the body of \(\sigma _\times = \mathtt{gmScalingP1}\) (215) is constructed: each per-chart scheme morphism (219) is built on chart \((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover})_i\), the cocycle (220) checks agreement on each pairwise pullback of cover charts, and the over-side coherence (222) intertwines the glued total morphism with the structure maps to \(\operatorname{Spec}\bar k\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 288Cross-chart intersection source of the scaling cover

The identification of the pullback of the two charts \((\mathtt{cover}.f\, 0)\), \((\mathtt{cover}.f\, 1)\) of 218 with \(\operatorname{Spec}\big((\mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, (X_0 X_1)) \otimes _{\bar k} \bar k[t, t^{-1}]\big)\), the cross chart over the merged degree-\(2\) generator \(X_0 X_1\). Built by the \(\mathtt{Proj.pullbackAwayιIso}\) route, mirroring 287.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 287Chart-\(i\) source of the scaling cover

The identification of the \(i\)-th chart of the scaling cover 218 with \(\operatorname{Spec}\big((\mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, (\! [X_0, X_1]\! )_i) \otimes _{\bar k} \bar k[t, t^{-1}]\big)\), built by composing \(\mathtt{pullbackSymmetry}\), \(\mathtt{pullbackRightPullbackFstIso}\), the 284 rewrite, and \(\mathtt{pullbackSpecIso}\). Used to define the chart map 219.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 268\(\mathbb G_m\) as a bare scheme

The underlying scheme of the multiplicative group \(\mathbb G_m\) over \(\operatorname{Spec}\bar k\): \(\operatorname{Spec}\bar k[t, t^{-1}]\) (267). This affine encoding (rather than the basic-open of \(\mathbb A^1\)) keeps \(\mathbb G_m\) affine. The object \(\mathbb G_m\) (193) is this scheme with its structure morphism.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 595

[Grassmannian scheme] Source: [Nitsure], §1, Exercise (2) and "Construction of Grassmannian" (FGA Explained Ch. 5). Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and let \(V\) be a locally free \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module of rank \(r\). For an integer \(1 \le d \le r\), the Grassmannian of rank-\(d\) quotients of \(V\) is the functor

\[ \mathrm{Grass}(V, d) : (\mathrm{Sch}/S)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathbf{Set}, \qquad T \longmapsto \{ \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle : q : V_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F},\; \mathcal{F} \text{ locally free of rank } d \} \]

with equivalence \(\ker (q) = \ker (q')\). Concretely \(\mathrm{Grass}(V, d) = \operatorname {Quot}^{d,\mathcal{O}_S}_{V/S/S}\) (the Quot functor for \(X = S\), \(E = V\), constant Hilbert polynomial \(\Phi = d\)).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 335

[(i.b-packaging) Adjunction-transpose compatibility of structure presheaves of rings along a scheme morphism]

Let \(f \colon Y \to Z\) be a morphism of schemes. The adjunction-transpose compatibility morphism along \(f\) packages the canonical morphism of presheaves of commutative rings on \(Y\)

\[ \varphi _f \; \colon \; f_{\mathrm{top}}^{-1}\, \mathcal O_Z \; \longrightarrow \; \mathcal O_Y, \]

obtained as the image of the structure-sheaf morphism \(f^{\sharp } \colon \mathcal O_Z \to (f_{\mathrm{top}})_{*}\, \mathcal O_Y\) under the symmetric direction of the presheaf-pullback / pushforward adjunction \(f_{\mathrm{top}}^{-1} \dashv (f_{\mathrm{top}})_{*}\) on \(\mathrm{CommRingCat}\)-valued presheaves of the underlying topological map \(f_{\mathrm{top}}\). This is the precise compatibility-of-rings shape consumed by the project’s relativeDifferentialsPresheaf on Lean targets whose ring-side input comes from a scheme morphism: the Differentials.lean:52 construction of \(\varphi '\) from \(f.c\) via the same adjunction transpose is the running model. The helper is used in piece (i.b) wherever a scheme morphism’s structure-presheaf data must be fed into relativeDifferentialsPresheaf in this transposed shape.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 635Existence carrier for the Abel map

The predicate \(\mathrm{HasAbelMap}(C)\) asserts the existence of a natural transformation of presheaves \(\mathrm{Div}_{C/k} \to \operatorname{Pic}^\sharp _{(C/k)\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}\), i.e. a candidate Abel map between the carrier functors of 633 and 630.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 632Existence carrier for the relative-divisor functor

The predicate \(\mathrm{HasDivFunctor}(C)\) asserts that the category of presheaves of types on \((\operatorname{Sch}/k)^{op}\) admits at least one object intended to be the relative-effective-divisor functor \(\mathrm{Div}_{C/k}\), sending a \(k\)-scheme \(T\) to the set of relative effective Cartier divisors on \(C \times _k T\) flat over \(T\). It is the existence carrier for \(\mathrm{Div}_{C/k}\) ahead of the A.2.b sibling chapter.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 639Existence carrier for the Picard scheme

The predicate \(\mathrm{HasPicScheme}(C)\) asserts that there exists a \(k\)-scheme \(X\) carrying a representability witness for \(\operatorname{Pic}^\sharp _{(C/k)\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}\); in other words, that the Picard scheme \(\operatorname{Pic}_{C/k}\) exists. It is the existence carrier from which 628 extracts the representing object.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 629Existence carrier for the relative Picard functor

The predicate \(\mathrm{HasPicSharp}(C)\) asserts that the category of presheaves of types on \((\operatorname{Sch}/k)^{op}\) admits at least one object intended to be the étale-sheafified relative Picard functor \(\operatorname{Pic}^\sharp _{(C/k)\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}\) of 19. It is the existence carrier that lets the public declarations of this chapter mention \(\operatorname{Pic}^\sharp _{(C/k)\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}\) before the A.1.c sibling chapter has committed to a specific construction.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 637Conclusion carrier for the smooth-proper quotient lemma

For a morphism \(\alpha : Z \to P\) of presheaves of types on \((\operatorname{Sch}/k)^{op}\), the predicate \(\mathrm{HasSmoothProperQuotient}(\alpha )\) asserts that the target \(P\) is representable. It packages the conclusion of the Altman–Kleiman smooth-proper quotient lemma 626 under its four standing hypotheses.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 142

[Higher direct image] Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, § Quasi-coherence of higher direct images (and the underlying Cohomology chapter). Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. For \(i \geq 0\) the \(i\)-th higher direct image \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) is the \(i\)-th right derived functor of the pushforward functor \(f_*\) applied to \(\mathcal{F}\). Concretely, \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) is the \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module obtained as the sheafification of the presheaf

\[ V \; \longmapsto \; H^i\bigl(f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr), \qquad V \subset S \text{ open}, \]

where \(H^i(-, -)\) denotes sheaf cohomology. For \(i = 0\) this recovers the ordinary pushforward \(R^0 f_*\mathcal{F} = f_*\mathcal{F}\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 593

[Hilbert polynomial] Source: [Nitsure], §1 (FGA Explained Ch. 5); cf. [Hartshorne], III.5.2. Let \(X \to S\) be a finite-type morphism of noetherian schemes and let \(L\) be a line bundle on \(X\). For a coherent sheaf \(\mathcal{F}\) on \(X\) whose schematic support is proper over \(S\) and a point \(s \in S\), the Hilbert polynomial of \(\mathcal{F}\) at \(s\) relative to \(L\) is the polynomial \(\Phi _{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb {Q}[\lambda ]\) characterised by

\[ \Phi _{\mathcal{F},s}(m) \; =\; \chi (X_s,\, \mathcal{F}|_{X_s} \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}) \; =\; \sum _{i \ge 0} (-1)^i \dim _{\kappa (s)} H^i(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}|_{X_s} \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}) \]

(a polynomial in \(m\) by Snapper’s Lemma; the sum is finite by coherence and properness). When \(\mathcal{F}\) is \(S\)-flat the function \(s \mapsto \Phi _{\mathcal{F},s}\) is locally constant on \(S\), so \(\Phi \) is well-defined on connected components.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 253Forward chart-ring map \(\mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, (X_i) \to \bar k[u]\)

The forward direction of the chart-ring iso (204): the ring map \(\mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, (X_i) \to \bar k[u]\) obtained from the chart evaluation 249 by \(\mathtt{Localization.awayLift}\) (legitimate because the evaluation sends \(X_i\) to the unit \(1\), 250), precomposed with the canonical map from the homogeneous localization to the ordinary localization.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 918Pole divisor of \(\varphi \colon C \to \mathbb P^1\)

Let \(C / \bar k\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve, regular in codimension one, and let \(\varphi \colon C \to \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) be a morphism, with \(K(C)\) carrying the canonical \(\varphi \)-induced \(K(\mathbb P^1)\)-algebra structure. The pole divisor of \(\varphi \) is the Weil divisor

\[ \varphi ^*[\infty ] \; :=\; \bigl(\operatorname {div}(\varphi ^\# t_\infty )\bigr)_0 \; =\; \operatorname {positivePart}\! \bigl(\operatorname {div}(\varphi ^\# t_\infty )\bigr) \; \in \; \operatorname{Div}(C), \]

the positive part of the principal divisor on \(C\) of the pulled-back local parameter \(\varphi ^\# t_\infty = \operatorname {algebraMap}_{K(\mathbb P^1) \to K(C)}(t_\infty )\), where \(t_\infty \) is the local parameter of 916. The pullback is nonzero because the algebra map \(K(\mathbb P^1) \to K(C)\) is injective (a ring homomorphism out of a field), so \(t_\infty \neq 0\) gives \(\varphi ^\# t_\infty \neq 0\) and the principal divisor is well defined. On a complete nonsingular curve the zeros of \(\varphi ^\# t_\infty \) are exactly the preimages of \(\infty \), so the positive part recovers \(\varphi ^*[\infty ]\) on the nose.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 655The carrier of the identity component

Let \(k\) be a field and \(G\) a \(k\)-group scheme locally of finite type. The carrier of the identity component is the open subset of \(|G|\) given by the connected component \(\operatorname {Comp}(e(\ast ))\) of the identity point (654), packaged as an open of \(G\); its openness is supplied by the local connectedness of \(|G|\) (653).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 645

[Identity component of a group scheme] Source: [Kleiman], “The Picard scheme”, §5, Lem. 5.1. Let \(k\) be a field and \(G\) a \(k\)-group scheme locally of finite type whose identity section \(e : \operatorname{Spec}k \to G\) factors through \(G\). The identity component of \(G\), denoted \(G^0\), is the locally closed subscheme of \(G\) whose underlying topological space is the connected component of \(|G|\) containing the image of \(e\), equipped with the reduced-induced (a priori) subscheme structure inherited from \(G\). In characteristic zero, or more generally when \(G\) has a geometrically reduced open subscheme, the substructure agrees with the open subscheme structure of 646.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 659The identity section into the identity component

Let \(k\) be a field and \(G\) a \(k\)-group scheme locally of finite type. The identity section \(e : \operatorname{Spec}k \to G\) factors through the open immersion \(G^0 \hookrightarrow G\) (655); the resulting morphism \(\operatorname{Spec}k \to G^0\) is the identity section into the identity component, obtained by the universal property of the open immersion using the range containment of 658.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 654The identity point

Let \(k\) be a field and \(G\) a \(k\)-group scheme. The identity point of \(G\) is the image \(e(\ast ) \in |G|\) of the unique point of \(\operatorname{Spec}k\) under the identity section \(e : \operatorname{Spec}k \to G\) (well-defined because \(|\operatorname{Spec}k|\) is a one-point space).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 672

[Indeterminacy locus of a rational map] Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, §I.3, p. 16. Let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map between varieties over \(\bar k\). The domain of definition of \(f\) is the open set

\[ \mathrm{Dom}(f) \; :=\; \bigcup _{(U, \varphi _U) \in f} U \; \subseteq \; X, \]

i.e. the union of the dense opens of all representatives of \(f\). The indeterminacy locus of \(f\) is the closed complement

\[ Z(f) \; :=\; X \smallsetminus \mathrm{Dom}(f) \; \subseteq \; X. \]

The map \(f\) is regular (i.e. defined on all of \(X\)) if and only if \(Z(f) = \emptyset \).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 871Canonical injective-embedding short complex

For a sheaf \(\mathcal F\) of \(\bar k\)-modules on \(X\), the canonical injective embedding \(\mathcal F \hookrightarrow \mathrm{Injective.under}\, \mathcal F\) and the cokernel projection onto its cokernel assemble into the short complex

\[ \mathcal F \; \longrightarrow \; \mathrm{Injective.under}\, \mathcal F \; \longrightarrow \; \mathrm{coker}\bigl(\mathcal F \hookrightarrow \mathrm{Injective.under}\, \mathcal F\bigr). \]

This is the short complex through which the Hartshorne III.2.5 induction runs.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 636Existence witness for the Abel map

The canonical instance recording that 635 holds for every such curve \(C/k\); the extracted natural transformation is the Abel map of 624. Its substantive discharge is analysed as Sorry 3 in 25.8.3.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 634Existence witness for the relative-divisor functor

The canonical instance recording that 632 holds for every such curve \(C/k\). Its substantive discharge is analysed as Sorry 2 in 25.8.2.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 640Existence witness for the Picard scheme

The canonical instance recording that 639 holds for every smooth proper geometrically integral curve \(C/k\). Its substantive discharge — the four-step FGA construction of 625, gated on the Riemann–Roch substrate — is analysed as Sorry 5 in 25.8.5.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 631Existence witness for the relative Picard functor

The canonical instance recording that 629 holds for every smooth proper geometrically integral curve \(C/k\). Its substantive discharge — exhibiting the actual étale sheafification rather than a tautological witness — is analysed as Sorry 1 in 25.8.1.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 638Existence witness for the smooth-proper quotient

The canonical instance recording that 637 holds for every morphism \(\alpha \) satisfying the hypotheses of 626. Its substantive discharge — the Altman–Kleiman effective-equivalence-relation descent together with the EGA IV 8.11.5 closed-immersion step — is analysed as Sorry 4 in 25.8.4.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 644Existence witness for the group-object structure

The canonical instance recording that 643 holds for every such curve \(C/k\); the extracted structure is the group scheme of 627. Its substantive discharge — the Yoneda transport of the tensor-product abelian-group operations — is analysed as Sorry 7 in 25.8.7.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 642Existence witness for the representability identification

The canonical instance recording that 641 holds for every such curve \(C/k\). Its substantive discharge is analysed as Sorry 6 in 25.8.6.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 534Open-by-open evaluation/contraction map

At an object \(X\), the \(R(X)\)-bilinear contraction \((M(X)) \otimes _{R(X)} (M|_X \to R|_X) \to R(X)\), \(s \otimes \varphi \mapsto \varphi (s)\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 530Underlying abelian-group presheaf of the internal hom

The \(\mathbf{Ab}\)-valued presheaf \(U \mapsto (M|_U \to N|_U)\) with the further-restriction maps, prior to the \(R(U)\)-module refinement.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 520Restriction of a presheaf of modules to the over-category

The restriction \(M|_U\) of the slice internal-hom formula: the pushforward of \(M\) along the over-category projection \(\mathtt{Over.forget}\, U\), whose base ring at the terminal \(\mathtt{Over.mk}\, (\mathrm{id}_U)\) is \(R(U)\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 299Chart-\(1\) section of \(\mathbb G_m\) into the scaling cover

The section \(s \colon \mathbb G_m \to (\sigma _\times \text{-cover}).X_1\) into the chart-\(1\) member of the scaling cover (218), built as a pullback lift with \(\mathbb G_m.\mathrm{hom} \mathbin {\, ;\, }r_1\) (293) as its chart component and the inner lift assembled from 298; its defining factorisation uses 294.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 293Chart-\(1\) lift of the point \([1:1]\)

The factor \(r_1 \colon \operatorname{Spec}\bar k \to \operatorname{Spec}(\mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, X_1)\) of the \(\bar k\)-point \([1:1] \in \mathbb P^1\) (195) through the chart-\(1\) open immersion \(\mathrm{Proj.away}\iota \, X_1\), produced by the universal property of the open immersion together with the range-containment witness 292.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 304

[Albanese universal property of a pointed curve] Source: Kleiman, The Picard Scheme, Remark (the Albanese map, rmk:Alb). Let \((C, P)\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over \(k\) with a marked \(k\)-rational point \(P \in C(k)\). A smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme \(J\) over \(k\) is an Albanese object for \((C, P)\) if there exists a morphism \(\iota \colon C \to J\) sending \(P\) to the identity element \(\eta \in J\), and such that every morphism \(f \colon C \to A\) from \(C\) to a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme \(A\) with \(f(P) = \eta _A\) factors uniquely as \(f = \iota \circ g\) for some morphism of group schemes \(g \colon J \to A\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 306

[The Albanese universal morphism]

Given a pointed curve \((C, P)\) and an Albanese term \(h \colon \mathrm{IsAlbanese}\, C\, P\, J\), the universal morphism of \(h\) is the morphism

\[ \iota := h.\mathtt{ofCurve} \colon C \to J, \]

obtained by Classical.choose on the existential body of \(h\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 859

[Flasque sheaf] Source: Hartshorne, II.1, Exercise 1.16 (definition); recalled III.2 preceding Lemma 2.4. Let \((X, \mathcal O_X)\) be a ringed space and let \(\mathcal F\) be a sheaf of \(\mathcal O_X\)-modules on \(X\). We say that \(\mathcal F\) is flasque if, for every inclusion of open sets \(V \subseteq U\) in \(X\), the restriction map \(\mathcal F(U) \to \mathcal F(V)\) is surjective.

In the project’s \(\mathrm{Module}\bar k\)-flavoured setup the predicate is applied to a sheaf \(\mathcal F : \mathrm{Sheaf}\; J\; (\mathrm{Module}\bar k)\), where \(J\) is the Grothendieck topology of opens on the underlying topological space of the curve \(C\). The surjectivity condition is read on the underlying presheaf of \(\bar k\)-modules; the project predicate Scheme.IsFlasque packages exactly this condition.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 383

[Local triviality of a \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module] Source: Stacks 01HH (locally trivial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules), specialised to rank one. An \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{M}\) on a scheme \(X\) is locally trivial of rank one if for every point \(x \in X\) there exists an affine open \(U \ni x\) and an iso \(\mathcal{M}|_U \cong \mathcal{O}_U\) (the structure sheaf restricted to \(U\) viewed as the unit object of \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\)). Formally:

\[ \forall \, x : X, \exists \, U : X.\mathrm{Opens}, x \in U \, \wedge \, \mathtt{IsAffineOpen}\, U \, \wedge \, \mathtt{Nonempty}\, (\mathcal{M}.\mathrm{restrict}\, U.\iota \cong \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\, \_ ). \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 791

[Regular in codimension one] Source: Hartshorne, II §6, p. 130 (the fourth clause of \((\ast )\)). A scheme \(X\) is regular in codimension one when for every point \(x \in X\) with \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{ x\} } = 1\) (equivalently, \(\texttt{Order.coheight}\, x = 1\)) the local ring \(\mathcal O_{X,x}\) is a discrete valuation ring. The Lean encoding bundles this conjunction as a one-field Prop class so that downstream order / principal / degree lemmas may consume it as a typeclass hypothesis.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 792DVR stalk from regularity in codimension one

Bridge instance: for an integral scheme \(X\) satisfying \(\texttt{IsRegularInCodimensionOne}\) (791) and any prime divisor \(Y : X.\texttt{PrimeDivisor}\) (789), the stalk \(\mathcal O_{X, Y.\texttt{point}}\) is a discrete valuation ring. This is the direct unpacking of the defining field of the class, exposed so that typeclass synthesis can supply the DVR structure at every codimension-one point.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 793Krull dimension \(\leq 1\) of a prime-divisor stalk

Bridge instance: for an integral scheme \(X\) satisfying \(\texttt{IsRegularInCodimensionOne}\) and any prime divisor \(Y : X.\texttt{PrimeDivisor}\), the stalk \(\mathcal O_{X, Y.\texttt{point}}\) has Krull dimension \(\leq 1\). This is the typeclass form consumed by the order definition (810), obtained from the DVR structure (792) via the chain “DVR \(\Rightarrow \) principal ideal ring \(\Rightarrow \) Krull dimension \(\leq 1\)”.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 311

[Jacobian]

The Jacobian of \(C\) is the abelian variety \(\mathrm{Jac}(C)\) over \(k\) obtained as the Albanese object of \(C\): a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over \(k\) of dimension \(g(C)\) equipped with a universal morphism \(C \to \mathrm{Jac}(C)\) (for each choice of marked point). It is viewed as an object of the category of \(k\)-schemes over \(\operatorname{Spec}k\). Formally, \(\mathrm{Jac}(C)\) is defined as the underlying scheme of a (uniform-over-\(P\)) Albanese witness for \(C\) provided by Theorem 319. When \(g(C) = 0\) the Jacobian collapses to \(\operatorname{Spec}k\) itself, which is the terminal object in the category of \(k\)-schemes. When \(g(C) {\gt} 0\) the underlying scheme is the abelian variety produced by Theorem 319; the genus-one case recovers \(C\) itself (after choosing a \(k\)-rational point), and the higher genus case yields the non-trivial \(g\)-dimensional abelian variety. Both cases are handled uniformly: \(\mathrm{Jac}(C)\) is the underlying scheme of the Albanese witness, with no special-case branching at the level of the definition. The genus-\(0\) specialisation is implicit in the fact that any group scheme over \(k\) of relative dimension \(0\) is isomorphic to \(\operatorname{Spec}k\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 778The Jacobian scheme \(\operatorname{Pic}^0_{C/k}\)

The Jacobian scheme \(J := \operatorname{Pic}^0_{C/k}\) of \(C\) is the underlying \(\bar k\)-scheme of the Pic\(^0\) bundle of 777.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 320Chosen Albanese witness

For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C\) over \(k\), \(\mathtt{jacobianWitness}\, C \colon \mathtt{JacobianWitness}\, C\) is a choice of Albanese witness for \(C\) (316), extracted from the non-emptiness 319 via Classical.choice. Its fields define the Jacobian \(\mathrm{Jac}(C)\) (311) and discharge each of the four protected abelian-variety instances on it (group object, smoothness of relative dimension \(g(C)\), properness, geometric irreducibility).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 316

[Albanese witness for a curve]

An Albanese witness for the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C\) over \(k\) is a bundle of the following seven fields:

  • \(J\): an object of the category of \(k\)-schemes over \(\operatorname{Spec}k\) — the candidate Albanese object.

  • \(\mathtt{grpObj}\): a group-object structure on \(J\) in the category of \(k\)-schemes over \(\operatorname{Spec}k\).

  • \(\mathtt{proper}\): a proof that the structure morphism \(J \to \operatorname{Spec}k\) is proper.

  • \(\mathtt{smooth}\): a proof that the structure morphism \(J \to \operatorname{Spec}k\) is smooth.

  • \(\mathtt{geomIrred}\): a proof that the structure morphism \(J \to \operatorname{Spec}k\) is geometrically irreducible.

  • \(\mathtt{smoothGenus}\): a proof that the structure morphism \(J \to \operatorname{Spec}k\) is smooth of relative dimension \(g(C)\). This refines \(\mathtt{smooth}\) at the specific relative dimension \(\mathrm{genus}\, C\) (see Remark 318).

  • \(\mathtt{isAlbaneseFor}\): a proof that, for every \(k\)-rational marked point \(P \in C(k)\), the scheme \(J\) together with its group-object, proper, smooth, and geometrically-irreducible structures satisfies the Albanese universal property for the pointed curve \((C, P)\) in the sense of Definition 304.

Formally, an Albanese witness is a Lean-level record (a structure) whose name is JacobianWitness.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 295Chart-\(1\) evaluation ring map \(\mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, X_1 \to \bar k\)

The \(\bar k\)-algebra evaluation map \(\mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, X_1 \to \mathrm{MvPolynomial}\, \{ \ast \} \, \bar k \to \bar k\), the composite of the chart-\(1\) ring iso \(\mathtt{homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly}\) (253, sending the canonical generator \(\mathtt{isLocElem}\) to the affine coordinate \(u\)) with evaluation of \(u\) at \(\lambda = 1\). This is the ring-map shadow of the point \([1:1]\) read in chart \(1\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 254Base ring map \(\bar k \to \mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, (X_i)\)

The composite \(\bar k \to \mathcal A_0 \to \mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, (X_i)\) of the algebra map into the degree-\(0\) piece with \(\mathtt{fromZeroRingHom}\); it carries the scalars of the inverse chart-ring map (255).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 846

[\(\ell \)-invariant of a Weil divisor]

Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, p. 295 (definition of \(l(D)\)). Let \(C\) be a smooth proper curve over \(\bar k\) and let \(D \in \mathrm{Div}(C)\) be a Weil divisor (790). The \(\ell \)-invariant of \(D\) is the integer

\[ \ell (D) \; :=\; \dim _{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(D)) \; \in \; \mathbb N, \]

the \(\bar k\)-dimension of the space of global sections of the invertible sheaf \(\mathcal O_C(D)\) associated to \(D\). Finiteness of \(\ell (D)\) is a consequence of the coherent-cohomology finiteness theorem on a proper \(\bar k\)-scheme (Hartshorne II.5.19 or III.5.2). The classical complete linear system \(|D|\) has \(\bar k\)-dimension \(\ell (D) - 1\) as a projective space, but the chapter never uses the projective-space interpretation; \(\ell (D)\) is the only quantity it needs.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 572Free–unit isomorphism

Over a sheaf of rings \(R\) on a site, the free \(R\)-module on a single generator is canonically isomorphic to the unit module:

\[ \mathtt{free}\, (\mathrm{PUnit}) \; \xrightarrow {\ \sim \ }\; \mathtt{unit}\, R, \]

the coproduct of a one-element family of copies of \(\mathtt{unit}\, R\) being \(\mathtt{unit}\, R\) itself.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 573Unit generating section

The canonical generating family of the unit module \(\mathtt{unit}\, R\): the single section indexed by \(\mathrm{PUnit}\), namely the image of the free generator under the free–unit isomorphism 572. The associated map \(\mathtt{unit}\, R^{\oplus 1} \to \mathtt{unit}\, R\) is an epimorphism, so the family generates.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 574Canonical unit presentation

The canonical finite free presentation of the unit module \(\mathtt{unit}\, R\): one generator (573) and no relations (indexed by the empty type). Since the generating map \(\mathtt{unit}\, R^{\oplus 1} \to \mathtt{unit}\, R\) is an isomorphism, its relation kernel is the zero object, so the empty family of relations suffices. This presentation is finite.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 382

[Line bundle on a product] Source: [Kleiman], “The Picard scheme”, §2, Def. “absolute Picard functor” (cf. Stacks tag 01HK, Hartshorne II §6). Let \(k\) be a base field, \(C\) a \(k\)-scheme (in our application, a smooth proper geometrically integral curve), and \(T\) a \(k\)-scheme. A line bundle on the relative curve \(C \times _k T\) is an invertible \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times _k T}\)-module, that is, an \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times _k T}\)-module \(\mathcal{L}\) such that there exists an open cover \(\{ U_i\} \) of \(C \times _k T\) on which each restriction \(\mathcal{L}|_{U_i}\) is free of rank one. Two line bundles are isomorphic if they are isomorphic as \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times _k T}\)-modules. The set of isomorphism classes forms an abelian group under tensor product (the Picard group), denoted \(\operatorname{Pic}(C \times _k T)\). In Kleiman’s notation with \(X = C\) and \(S = \operatorname{Spec}k\), this is exactly the value \(\operatorname{Pic}_C(T) = \operatorname{Pic}(C \times _k T)\) of the absolute Picard functor.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 844

[Linear equivalence of divisors] Source: Hartshorne, II.6, p. 131 (linear equivalence and \(\operatorname {Cl} X\)). Let \(X\) satisfy \((*)\). Two Weil divisors \(D, D' \in \mathrm{Div}(X)\) are linearly equivalent, written \(D \sim D'\), if and only if there exists \(f \in K(X)^{\times }\) with

\[ D - D' \; =\; \mathrm{div}(f) \; \in \; \mathrm{Div}(X). \]

The relation \(\sim \) is an equivalence relation on \(\mathrm{Div}(X)\) (the reflexivity, symmetry, and transitivity all transport from the analogous identities \(\mathrm{div}(1) = 0\), \(\mathrm{div}(f^{-1}) = -\mathrm{div}(f)\), \(\mathrm{div}(fg) = \mathrm{div}(f) + \mathrm{div}(g)\) via 833); the corresponding quotient group \(\operatorname {Cl}(X) := \mathrm{Div}(X) / \mathrm{im}(\div )\) is the divisor class group of \(X\). On a smooth proper curve \(C\) over \(\bar k\), 834 implies that the degree map \(\deg \colon \mathrm{Div}(C) \to \mathbb Z\) descends to a homomorphism \(\deg \colon \operatorname {Cl}(C) \to \mathbb Z\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 569Engine bridge: line-bundle over–restrict trivialisation

The line-bundle engine’s local over–restrict trivialisation bridge: given \(M \in \mathrm{Scheme}.\mathtt{Modules}\, X\), an open \(U \subseteq X\), and a trivialisation \(e : M|_\iota \xrightarrow {\sim } \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\, (\widetilde{U}).\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\), it produces the slice-level isomorphism \(M.\mathtt{over}\, U \xrightarrow {\sim } \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\, (X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}.\mathtt{over}\, U)\). It is the consumer-facing alias used by the finite-presentation engine, defined to be precisely the general construction 568.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 895

[Line bundle of a closed point]

Source: Hartshorne, II.6, p. 144 (definition of \(\mathscr L(D)\) and Proposition 6.13(a)). Let \(C\) be a smooth proper curve over \(\bar k\) and let \(P \in C\) be a closed point. Let \(\mathfrak m_P \subset \mathcal O_{C, P}\) denote the maximal ideal of the local ring at \(P\); by smoothness of \(C\), \(\mathcal O_{C, P}\) is a discrete valuation ring and \(\mathfrak m_P\) is generated by any uniformizer \(f_P \in \mathcal O_{C, P}\) (an element of \(\mathfrak m_P \setminus \mathfrak m_P^2\)). The Cartier divisor associated to the closed point \(P\) is the effective Cartier divisor cut out locally by the uniformizer \(f_P\) on an open affine neighbourhood of \(P\), and by the unit \(1\) on the complement \(C \setminus \{ P\} \). The line bundle of the closed point \(P\), denoted \(\mathcal O_C(P)\), is the locally free \(\mathcal O_C\)-module of rank \(1\) defined by Hartshorne’s construction \(\mathscr L(D)\) applied to this Cartier divisor: explicitly, \(\mathcal O_C(P)\) is the sub-\(\mathcal O_C\)-module of the sheaf of total quotient rings \(\mathcal K_C\) (the constant sheaf of the function field \(K(C)\) on the integral scheme \(C\)) that is generated by \(f_P^{-1} \in K(C)\) on a neighbourhood of \(P\) and by \(1 \in K(C)\) on the complement \(C \setminus \{ P\} \). Hartshorne’s Proposition 6.13(a) confirms that this subsheaf is an invertible sheaf, independently of the choice of uniformizer \(f_P\) (any two uniformizers differ by a unit in \(\mathcal O_{C, P}^{\times }\), so they generate the same \(\mathcal O_C\)-submodule of \(\mathcal K_C\)).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 892

[Presheaf carrier for the line bundle of a closed point] For each open \(U \subseteq C\), the \(\bar k\)-submodule \(\mathrm{carrierSubmodule}_P(U) \subset K(C)\) of section candidates satisfying the order conditions “\(\mathrm{ord}_Q f \ge 0\) for all prime divisors \(Q \in U\) other than \(P\), and additionally \(\mathrm{ord}_P f \ge -1\) when \(P \in U\)” assembles into a \(\bar k\)-linear functor

\[ \texttt{carrierPresheaf}_P : (\mathrm{Opens}\, |C|)^{\mathrm{op}} \longrightarrow \mathrm{ModuleCat}_{\bar k}, \qquad U \longmapsto \mathrm{carrierSubmodule}_P(U), \]

with restriction \(V \subseteq U \mapsto \texttt{Submodule.inclusion}\, (\texttt{carrierSet\_ mono})\) on each pair. The functor laws follow from the antitone monotonicity \(U \mapsto \mathrm{carrierSet}_P(U)\) (witness \(\texttt{carrierSet\_ mono}\) at L181-192 of the Lean file) by \(\texttt{ext}\, \langle x, \_ \rangle \); rfl.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 884Carrier set of the line bundle of a closed point over an open

Let \(C\) be an integral, locally Noetherian, regular-in-codimension-one scheme over \(\bar k\), let \(P \in C\) be a point of coheight \(1\), and let \(U \subseteq C\) be an open set. The carrier set \(\mathrm{carrierSet}_P(U) \subseteq K(C)\) is the set of rational functions \(f\) satisfying the order conditions \(\operatorname {ord}_Q(f) \ge 0\) for every prime divisor \(Q\) with \(Q \in U\) and \(Q \ne P\), together with \(\operatorname {ord}_P(f) \ge -1\) whenever \(P \in U\). It is the underlying set of the section module of \(\mathcal O_C(P)\) over \(U\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 888Carrier submodule of the line bundle of a closed point

With \(C, P, U\) as in 884, the carrier set \(\mathrm{carrierSet}_P(U)\) is a \(\bar k\)-submodule of \(K(C)\). It contains \(0\); it is closed under addition by the non-archimedean inequality \(\operatorname {ord}_Q(f + g) \ge \min (\operatorname {ord}_Q f, \operatorname {ord}_Q g)\) for the discrete valuation at each regular codimension-one stalk; and it is closed under scaling by a constant \(c \in \bar k\), since \(c\) maps to a unit in every stalk and hence has order \(0\) at every prime divisor.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 894

[Carrier-submodule refinement enforcing \(F(\bot )=0\)] Iter-188 Lane A discovery: the raw \(\mathrm{carrierSubmodule}_P\) of 892 satisfies \(\mathrm{carrierSubmodule}_P(\bot ) = K(C)\) (since the order conditions are vacuously satisfied on the empty open). This violates the sheaf axiom on the Grothendieck topology of opens: the empty sieve covers \(\bot \), forcing \(F(\bot ) =\) terminal \(= 0\) on \(\mathrm{ModuleCat}_{\bar k}\)-valued functors. The refinement

\[ \mathrm{carrierSubmoduleSheaf}_P(U) \; :=\; \mathrm{carrierSubmodule}_P(U) \; \sqcap \; \mathrm{trivAtBot}\, U, \qquad \mathrm{trivAtBot}\, U \; :=\; \{ \, f \; \mid \; U \ne \bot \, \lor \, f = 0 \, \} . \]

forces \(F(\bot ) = \bot \) (intersection annihilates) while preserving \(F(U)\) on non-empty \(U\) (since then \(\mathrm{trivAtBot}\, U = \top \)). The case-based restriction map (zero target on \(\bot \); inclusion otherwise) gives a well-formed sheaf-condition substrate consumed by 893. Downstream consumers lineBundleAtClosedPoint, toFunctionField, globalSections_iff_* are updated to supply the trivAtBot witness at \(\mathrm{op}\, \top \) via the htop_ne_bot helper.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 891Type-level subfunctor presentation of the carrier

With \(C, P\) as above, the carrier of \(\mathcal O_C(P)\) is presented as a subfunctor of the type-valued presheaf \(U \mapsto (U \to K(C))\) of arbitrary \(K(C)\)-valued functions: a section over \(U\) is a constant function with value \(f \in \mathrm{carrierSubmoduleSheaf}_P(U)\). This presentation reduces the sheaf condition to a stalk-locality check against the ambient sheaf of type-valued functions, exploiting that any two nonempty opens of the irreducible scheme \(C\) intersect.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 887The \(\bar k\)-algebra structure on the function field

For an integral scheme \(C\) over \(\bar k\), the function field \(K(C)\) carries a \(\bar k\)-algebra structure, obtained by composing the algebra map \(\bar k \to \Gamma (C, \top )\) with the canonical map \(\Gamma (C, \top ) \to K(C)\). This structure is what makes \(K(C)\) a \(\bar k\)-module, so that the carrier sets can be upgraded to \(\bar k\)-submodules.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 886Nonemptiness of the top open of an integral scheme

For an integral scheme \(X\), the top open \(\top \subseteq X\) is nonempty as a scheme: its underlying set is all of \(X\), and \(X\) is nonempty since an integral scheme is irreducible. This instance lets the standard algebra map \(\Gamma (X, U) \to K(X)\) fire at \(U = \top \).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 896

[Inclusion of \(\mathcal O_C(P)\) into the function field]

Source: Hartshorne, II.6, p. 144 (definition of \(\mathscr L(D)\) as a subsheaf of \(\mathscr K\)). Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over \(\bar k\) with \(C\) integral, and let \(P \in C\) be a closed point. The line bundle \(\mathcal O_C(P)\) of 895 is by construction a sub-\(\mathcal O_C\)-module of the sheaf of total quotient rings \(\mathcal K_C\). Since \(C\) is integral, \(\mathcal K_C\) is the constant sheaf at the function field \(K(C)\), and taking global sections of the structural inclusion \(\mathcal O_C(P) \hookrightarrow \mathcal K_C\) produces a canonical \(\bar k\)-linear map

\[ \iota \; \colon \; H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) \; \longrightarrow \; H^0(C, \mathcal K_C) \; \cong \; K(C) \]

sending a global section \(s \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P))\) to the rational function on \(C\) obtained by composing \(s\) with the inclusion of \(\mathcal O_C(P)\) into \(\mathcal K_C\) at each point. The map \(\iota \) is injective (the sub-\(\mathcal O_C\)-module \(\mathcal O_C(P) \subset \mathcal K_C\) is torsion-free of rank \(1\), so taking global sections preserves injectivity on the integral scheme \(C\)).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 889Bot-trivialisation submodule

For an integral scheme \(C\) over \(\bar k\) and an open \(U \subseteq C\), the \(\bar k\)-submodule \(\mathrm{trivAtBot}(U) \subseteq K(C)\) equals all of \(K(C)\) when \(U \ne \emptyset \) and equals \(\{ 0\} \) when \(U = \emptyset \). Intersecting the carrier submodule with this factor enforces the sheaf-at-\(\emptyset \) condition \(F(\emptyset ) = 0\) required by the Grothendieck topology of opens.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 916Local parameter at \(\infty \in \mathbb P^1\)

Let \(\bar k\) be a field and present the projective line as \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} = \operatorname {Proj} \bar k[X_0, X_1]\). The local parameter at \(\infty \) is the rational function

\[ t_\infty \; :=\; X_0 / X_1 \; \in \; K(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}), \]

the degree-\(0\) ratio coordinate of the homogeneous localisation \(\bigl(\bar k[X_0, X_1]_{(X_1)}\bigr)_0 = \mathcal O\! \bigl(D_+(X_1)\bigr)\), transported into the function field \(K(\mathbb P^1)\) along the germ map on the affine chart \(D_+(X_1)\). On the Lean side it is packaged as a nonzero element of \(K(\mathbb P^1)\): the witness \(t_\infty \neq 0\) comes from the injectivity of the chart isomorphism \(\mathcal O\! \bigl(D_+(X_1)\bigr) \cong \bar k[X_0/X_1]\) (a \(\operatorname {Proj}\)-chart presentation) composed with the injective germ-to-function-field map of the integral scheme \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\), applied to the nonzero ratio element \(X_0 / X_1\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 239Canonical generators of \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\, \alpha \, R\)

The canonical \(\mathtt{Algebra.Generators}\) of \(R[\alpha ] = \mathrm{MvPolynomial}\, \alpha \, R\) over \(R\), indexed by \(\alpha \), with generating family \(\alpha \ni s \mapsto X_s\) and section the identity (\(\mathtt{aeval}\, X\) is the identity on \(R[\alpha ]\)).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 240Relation-free presentation of \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\, \alpha \, R\)

The canonical \(\mathtt{Algebra.Presentation}\) of \(R[\alpha ]\) over \(R\) extending 239 with an empty relation index, the kernel of \(\mathtt{aeval}\, X\) being \(\bot \) since \(\mathtt{aeval}\, X\) is the identity.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 241Pre-submersive presentation of \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\, \alpha \, R\)

The canonical \(\mathtt{Algebra.PreSubmersivePresentation}\) of \(R[\alpha ]\) over \(R\) extending 240; the differential map is on the empty (zero) relation module.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 242Submersive presentation of \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\, \alpha \, R\)

The canonical \(\mathtt{Algebra.SubmersivePresentation}\) of \(R[\alpha ]\) over \(R\) extending 241; the Jacobian is the determinant of the \(0 \times 0\) matrix, namely \(1\), a unit. This is the witness from which 200 reads off standard smoothness of relative dimension \(n\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 255Inverse chart-ring map \(\bar k[u] \to \mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, (X_i)\)

The inverse direction of the chart-ring iso: the ring map \(\bar k[u] \to \mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, (X_i)\) given by the universal property of \(\bar k[u]\) over the base map 254, sending the generator \(u\) to the degree-\(0\) element \(X_{\mathrm{other}\, i}/X_i\) (the canonical localization element \(\mathtt{isLocalizationElem}\)).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 361

[Abel–Jacobi map]

Let \(P \colon \operatorname{Spec}k \to C\) be a \(k\)-rational point of \(C\), equivalently a morphism \(P \colon \mathbf1 \to C\) in the category of \(k\)-schemes over \(\operatorname{Spec}k\). By Theorem 319 the Jacobian \(\mathrm{Jac}(C)\) of Definition 311 carries, for each such marked point \(P\), an Albanese structure \(\mathrm{IsAlbanese}\, C\, P\, \mathrm{Jac}(C)\) in the sense of Definition 304. The Abel–Jacobi map at \(P\) is the universal pointed morphism \(\iota _P\) supplied by that Albanese structure:

\[ \alpha _P \; :=\; \iota _P \; \colon \; C \; \longrightarrow \; \mathrm{Jac}(C). \]

Equivalently, \(\alpha _P\) is the projection of the universal-pointed-morphism field of the Albanese predicate \(\mathrm{IsAlbanese}\, C\, P\, \mathrm{Jac}(C)\), evaluated at the marked point \(P\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 385Underlying sheaf of a line bundle on the product

The underlying sheaf of modules of a line bundle \(\mathcal{L} \in \mathrm{OnProduct}\, \pi _C\, \pi _T\) (382) is its first component, the \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times _k T}\)-module \(\mathcal{L}.\mathrm{carrier}\) obtained by forgetting the local-triviality witness. Since \(\mathrm{OnProduct}\, \pi _C\, \pi _T\) is the subtype \(\{ \, \mathcal{M} : (C \times _k T).\mathrm{Modules} \mid \mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\, \mathcal{M}\, \} \) of 382, the carrier is the underlying \((C \times _k T).\mathrm{Modules}\) value, recovering the all-modules carrier of 382 by projecting away the second (invertibility) component.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 810

[Order of a rational function at a prime divisor]

Source: Hartshorne, II.6, pp. 130–131 (valuation \(v_Y\)). Let \(X\) satisfy \((*)\). For a prime divisor \(Y \subset X\) with generic point \(\eta \in Y\), the local ring \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta }\) is a discrete valuation ring (by regularity in codimension one) with quotient field the function field \(K(X)\). For a nonzero rational function \(f \in K(X)^{\times }\) the order of \(f\) along \(Y\) is the integer

\[ \operatorname {ord}_Y(f) \; :=\; v_Y(f) \; \in \; \mathbb Z, \]

where \(v_Y\) is the normalised discrete valuation associated to \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta }\). If \(\operatorname {ord}_Y(f) {\gt} 0\), \(f\) has a zero of order \(\operatorname {ord}_Y(f)\) along \(Y\); if \(\operatorname {ord}_Y(f) {\lt} 0\), \(f\) has a pole of order \(-\operatorname {ord}_Y(f)\) along \(Y\). Specialisation to a curve. If \(C\) is a smooth proper curve over \(\bar k\) and \(P \in C\) is a closed point, then \(P\) is automatically a prime divisor (it is closed, integral, of codimension one in the \(1\)-dimensional scheme \(C\)); the local ring \(\mathcal O_{C, P}\) is a discrete valuation ring by smoothness, and \(\operatorname {ord}_P(f) = v_P(f)\) is the standard DVR valuation of \(f\) at \(P\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 245The “other” \(\mathrm{Fin}\, 2\) index

The involution \(\mathrm{Fin}\, 2 \to \mathrm{Fin}\, 2\) swapping \(0\) and \(1\); it names the complementary coordinate \(X_{\mathrm{other}\, i}/X_i\) in the chart-\(i\) affine coordinate.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 558Continuity of the functor leg of the over-equivalence

The functor leg \(e.\mathtt{functor} : \mathtt{Over}\, U \to \mathtt{Opens}\, \widetilde{U}\) of the reindexing site equivalence is continuous from the slice topology \((\mathtt{Opens.grothendieckTopology}\, X).\mathtt{over}\, U\) to the subspace topology \(\mathtt{Opens.grothendieckTopology}\, \widetilde{U}\). It is the second continuity prerequisite of \(\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence}\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 557Continuity of the inverse leg of the over-equivalence

The inverse leg \(e.\mathtt{inverse} : \mathtt{Opens}\, \widetilde{U} \to \mathtt{Over}\, U\) of the reindexing site equivalence \(e = \mathtt{Opens.overEquivalence}\, U\) is continuous from the subspace topology \(\mathtt{Opens.grothendieckTopology}\, \widetilde{U}\) to the slice topology \((\mathtt{Opens.grothendieckTopology}\, X).\mathtt{over}\, U\). This is the continuity prerequisite, recorded for the scheme-carrier presentation of the open subscheme, that \(\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence}\) demands of the inverse leg.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 555Dense-subsite datum for the slice-site equivalence

The inverse of \(\mathtt{overEquivalence}\, U\) exhibits the opens of the subspace \(\widetilde{U}\) as a dense subsite of the slice site over \(U\); the only non-formal field is the pointwise cover-correspondence.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 565Index reconciliation natural isomorphism

The natural isomorphism

\[ \mathtt{Over.forget}\, U \; \xrightarrow {\ \sim \ }\; e.\mathtt{functor} \; \circ \; \iota _{\mathtt{opensFunctor}} \]

reconciling the two index functors \(\mathtt{Over}\, U \to \mathtt{Opens}\, X\) entering the composite pushforward; both send \(V \mapsto V.\mathrm{left}\), and the component at \(V\) is the \(\mathtt{eqToIso}\) of the open equality 559.
