Lemma 279Left round-trip of the representability bijection

\(\text{invFun} \circ \text{toFun} = \mathrm{id}\) on \(\mathrm{Hom}(T, \mathbb G_m)\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 280Right round-trip of the representability bijection

\(\text{toFun} \circ \text{invFun} = \mathrm{id}\) on \(\Gamma (T_{\mathrm{left}}, \top )^\times \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 273\(\bar k[t, t^{-1}]\) is an integral domain

The ring \(\bar k[t, t^{-1}]\) is an integral domain.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 220

[Cocycle agreement for \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ chart}\) on pairwise overlaps]

For every pair of chart indices \(x, y \in \mathrm{Fin}\, 2\) the two restrictions of \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ chart}\, \bar k\) (219) to the pairwise pullback \(\mathrm{pullback}\, ((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover})_x.f)\, ((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover})_y.f)\) agree:

\[ \mathrm{pullback.fst}\, ((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover})_x.f)\, ((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover})_y.f) \mathbin {\, ;\, }\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ chart}\, \bar k\, x \ = \mathrm{pullback.snd}\, ((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover})_x.f)\, ((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover})_y.f) \mathbin {\, ;\, }\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ chart}\, \bar k\, y. \]

This is the cocycle / overlap-coherence equation that \(\mathtt{Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms}\) consumes in order to assemble the chart-wise pieces \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ chart}\, \bar k\, i\) into a single morphism on the total \((\mathbb P^1 \otimes \mathbb G_m).\mathrm{left}\). The diagonal cases \((x, y) = (0, 0)\) and \((x, y) = (1, 1)\) are immediate via \(\mathtt{pullback.condition}\); the substantive content is the cross case \((0, 1)\) (and its symmetric \((1, 0)\)), broken down below. (I) Setup and structural lift. Restricting the indices to the cross case \((x, y) = (0, 1)\), the two charts being compared are \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ chart}\, \bar k\, 0\) over the cover patch \((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover}\, \bar k).X\, 0 = D_+(X_0) \times \mathbb G_m\) (containing \(0 \in \mathbb A^1\)) and \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ chart}\, \bar k\, 1\) over \((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover}\, \bar k).X\, 1 = D_+(X_1) \times \mathbb G_m\) (containing \(\infty \)). Their pairwise pullback intersection sits over the basic-open intersection \(D_+(X_0 \cdot X_1) \times \mathbb G_m\), and the project records an explicit iso identifying the intersection scheme with an affine \(\operatorname{Spec}\):

\[ \mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover\_ intersection\_ X\_ iso}\, \bar k \colon \mathrm{pullback}\, ((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover}\, \bar k)_0.f)\, ((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover}\, \bar k)_1.f) \xrightarrow {\ \sim \ } \operatorname{Spec}\bigl(\mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, (X_0 \cdot X_1) \otimes _{\bar k} \mathtt{GmRing}\, \bar k\bigr). \]

The cleanest entry point is to post-compose both sides of the cocycle with the iso’s inverse and cancel the resulting epimorphism — the \(\mathtt{cancel\_ epi}\, (\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover\_ intersection\_ X\_ iso}\, \bar k).\mathrm{inv}\) move. This lifts the agreement to a morphism equality between two arrows \(\operatorname{Spec}\bigl(\mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, (X_0 \cdot X_1) \otimes _{\bar k} \mathtt{GmRing}\, \bar k\bigr) \rightrightarrows \overline{\mathbb P^1}\), where both sides should factor through the merged-generator chart \(\mathrm{Proj.awayι}\, (X_0 \cdot X_1)\). The cocycle then reduces to an equality of two ring maps into \(\mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, (X_0 \cdot X_1) \otimes _{\bar k} \mathtt{GmRing}\, \bar k\). (II) Why the canonical simp chain is blocked. After \(\mathtt{cancel\_ epi}\) the goal contains the inverse of \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover\_ intersection\_ X\_ iso}\) composed with each pullback projection. In principle the Mathlib-canonical simp set \(\{ \mathtt{Iso.trans\_ inv},\, \mathtt{Iso.inv\_ comp\_ eq},\, \mathtt{pullback.congrHom\_ inv},\, \mathtt{asIso\_ inv},\, \mathtt{pullbackRightPullbackFstIso\_ inv\_ *},\, \mathtt{pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso\_ inv\_ *},\, \mathtt{pullbackSymmetry\_ inv\_ comp\_ *},\, \mathtt{Proj.pullbackAwayιIso\_ inv\_ *}\} \) together with the project-side simp helpers \(\mathtt{pullback\_ map\_ fst\_ proj}\) / \(\mathtt{pullback\_ map\_ snd\_ proj}\) (216, 217; iter-184 Recipe 1) would collapse the iso projections to a single \(\operatorname{Spec}.\mathrm{map}\) of a known ring map. Empirically, however, \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover\_ intersection\_ X\_ iso}\) is produced by a tactic-mode \(\mathtt{refine\ \ldots \ \mathbin {\, ;\, }\ \ldots \ \mathbin {\, ;\, }\ ?\_ }\) construction whose elaboration does not present its \(\mathtt{Iso.trans}\)-spine syntactically; consequently none of the canonical inv-projection simp lemmas matches the elaborated form, and the iter-181 through iter-185 attempts at closure via \(\mathtt{simp [Iso.trans\_ inv, \ldots ]}\) report every simp argument “unused”. This is the precise diagnostic recorded in \(\mathtt{analogies/gmscaling{\text-}projection{\text-}idiom.md}\) (iter-184 analogist consult, which supersedes the now-stale \(\mathtt{analogies/chart{\text-}bridge.md}\) cited in earlier iterations of this proof sketch). (III) Three concrete pickup paths, with iter-187 status. Three orthogonal strategies have been considered for closing this lemma. As of iter-187 the first two are off the table for the reasons recorded below, and only the third (III.c) — the separated-locus alternative — is the live route. The three-way enumeration is preserved so that future iterations have an audit trail of what has and has not been tried.

(III.a) Refactor the iso to term-mode \(\mathbin {\, ;\, }\)-spine — BLOCKED at Mathlib commit b80f227.

The original idea was to rewrite \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover\_ intersection\_ X\_ iso}\) as a single \(\mathtt{Iso.trans}\) (\(\mathbin {\, ;\, }\)-)chain of named Mathlib isos (\(\mathtt{pullbackRightPullbackFstIso}\), \(\mathtt{pullback.congrHom}\), \(\mathtt{pullbackSymmetry}\), \(\mathtt{Proj.pullbackAwayιIso}\), and the final \(\mathtt{pullbackSpecIso}\)), with no intermediate \(\mathtt{refine \ldots ?\_ }\) holes discharged in tactic mode, so that \(\mathtt{Iso.trans\_ inv}\) and the canonical inv-projection simp lemmas would unfold the chain link-by-link. Why this is blocked. The shim \(\mathtt{Algebra.TensorProduct.isDomain\_ of\_ isAlgClosed\_ left}\) (the bridge “factor algebraically closed \(+\) other a domain \(\Rightarrow \) tensor a domain”) on which this path’s product-stability instances rest is not present at the pinned Mathlib commit b80f227. Independently, the iter-186 Lane B prover empirically verified that even with the term-mode refactor in place, the cocycle’s residual simp normal form cannot be reduced by Mathlib’s existing \(\mathtt{pullbackRightPullbackFstIso\_ inv\_ *}\) family: those simp lemmas require the projection to be syntactically displayed at the top level, whereas in the elaborated cocycle the projections are buried inside outer \(\mathtt{pullback.map}\) constructs (\(\mathtt{simp}\) reports “made no progress”). Both obstacles are external to the project and require upstream Mathlib motion. The route is therefore permanently blocked at this commit pending Mathlib upstream.

(III.b) Package named projection lemmas via \(\mathtt{pullback.hom\_ ext}\) — DESCOPED iter-187.

The fallback was, without refactoring the iso, to prove two \(\mathtt{@[reassoc (attr := simp)]}\) lemmas \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover\_ intersection\_ X\_ iso\_ inv\_ fst}\) / \(\mathtt{\_ inv\_ snd}\) whose body is a hand-written \(\mathtt{pullback.hom\_ ext}\) against the relevant pullback projection of the intersection. The universal property of the pullback would force each projection’s value diagram-wise, sidestepping the opaque elaborated iso form because \(\mathtt{pullback.hom\_ ext}\) does not require syntactic unfolding. Why this is descoped this iteration. The iter-186 Lane B prover empirically determined that the (III.a) refactor (term-mode iso) does remove the elaboration-shape obstacle on the outer iso, but the simp coverage gap persists in mid-chain: the \(\mathtt{pullback.map} \mathbin {\, ;\, }\mathtt{pullbackRightPullbackFstIso}.\mathtt{inv}\) adjacency that the cocycle surfaces is not Mathlib-canonical, so even with the named projection lemmas built, the residual goal does not collapse without further Mathlib motion. The iter-185 STRATEGY.md Open Q “failure-mode trigger” fired in iter-186, and the iter-187 progress-critic returned a STUCK + OVER_BUDGET verdict against this lemma (sorry count flat 4/4/4/4 across iter-183 to iter-186, four consecutive iterations of pivot/recipe language with no sorry closure, mandatory-decrement gate missed iter-186). Accordingly this path is descoped for iter-187 to focus all Lane B effort on the alternative (III.c); it may be revisited only if (III.c) itself surfaces unexpected blockers.

(III.c) Separated-locus alternative — iter-187 MANDATORY PIVOT (per the iter-185 STRATEGY.md Open Q failure-mode trigger, the iter-187 progress-critic STUCK verdict, and the iter-187 blueprint-reviewer MF-2 directive).

The chart-agreement equation \(\mathrm{pullback.fst} \mathbin {\, ;\, }\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ chart}\, \bar k\, 0 = \mathrm{pullback.snd} \mathbin {\, ;\, }\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ chart}\, \bar k\, 1\) on the cover intersection \(\mathrm{pullback}\, ((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover})_0.f)\, ((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover})_1.f)\) is the statement that two morphisms \(\mathrm{chart}_0, \mathrm{chart}_1 \colon \mathrm{(intersection)} \to \overline{\mathbb P^1}\) agree on that intersection scheme. The key observation is that \(\overline{\mathbb P^1}\) is separated over \(\bar k\), so the diagonal \(\Delta _{\overline{\mathbb P^1}} \colon \overline{\mathbb P^1} \longrightarrow \overline{\mathbb P^1} \times _{\bar k} \overline{\mathbb P^1}\) is a closed immersion. Because \(\overline{\mathbb P^1} = \operatorname {Proj}(\bar k[X_0, X_1])\) is the \(\operatorname {Proj}\) of a graded \(\bar k\)-algebra, its separatedness over \(\operatorname{Spec}\bar k\) is the standard Stacks fact “\(\operatorname {Proj}(S)\) is separated” (Stacks tag 01KU / lemma lemma-proj-separated); Mathlib supplies the categorical packaging via \(\mathtt{IsSeparated}\) and \(\mathtt{IsSeparated.diagonal\_ isClosedImmersion}\). Bypass. Reformulate the cocycle as: the pair-morphism \((\mathrm{chart}_0, \mathrm{chart}_1) \colon \mathrm{(intersection)} \longrightarrow \overline{\mathbb P^1} \times _{\bar k} \overline{\mathbb P^1}\) (built via \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Limits.prod.lift}\) on \(\mathrm{chart}_0 := \mathrm{pullback.fst} \mathbin {\, ;\, }\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ chart}\, \bar k\, 0\) and \(\mathrm{chart}_1 := \mathrm{pullback.snd} \mathbin {\, ;\, }\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ chart}\, \bar k\, 1\)) factors through the diagonal \(\Delta _{\overline{\mathbb P^1}}\). By Mathlib’s \(\mathtt{IsClosedImmersion.lift\_ iff\_ range\_ subset}\) (the universal property of a closed immersion: a morphism factors through a closed immersion iff its set-theoretic image is contained in the image of the immersion), the factorization is equivalent to the set-level containment of the cocycle’s image in the image of \(\Delta \), which on points is exactly the chart equality on the closed points of the intersection. The closed-point check is discrete (each closed point of the intersection lies over a single \(\bar k\)- point of \(D_+(X_0 \cdot X_1) \times \mathbb G_m\) and the two chart maps both evaluate to the same \(\bar k\)-point of \(\overline{\mathbb P^1}\) corresponding to that \((x, \lambda )\)), and bypasses the simp coverage gap that obstructs (III.a)/(III.b) entirely. Concrete prover recipe. The path closes in four steps:

  1. Fix the diagonal \(\Delta _{\overline{\mathbb P^1}} \colon \overline{\mathbb P^1} \longrightarrow \overline{\mathbb P^1} \times _{\bar k} \overline{\mathbb P^1}\) via Mathlib’s \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Limits.prod.lift}\, (\mathbf1_{\overline{\mathbb P^1}})\, (\mathbf1_{\overline{\mathbb P^1}})\), and obtain the closed-immersion witness from \(\mathtt{IsSeparated.diagonal\_ isClosedImmersion}\) applied to the separated structure morphism \(\overline{\mathbb P^1}.\mathrm{hom} \colon \overline{\mathbb P^1} \to \operatorname{Spec}\bar k\).

  2. Build the pair-morphism \((\mathrm{chart}_0, \mathrm{chart}_1) \colon \mathrm{pullback}\, ((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover})_0.f)\, ((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover})_1.f) \longrightarrow \overline{\mathbb P^1} \times _{\bar k} \overline{\mathbb P^1}\) via \(\mathtt{prod.lift}\) on the two named chart restrictions above.

  3. Show this pair factors through \(\Delta _{\overline{\mathbb P^1}}\) via the universal property of the intersection’s pullback structure combined with \(\mathtt{IsClosedImmersion.lift}\) (kernel-inequality form; the \(\mathtt{\_ iff\_ range\_ subset}\) variant is NOT in Mathlib at b80f227 per iter-188 verification, so this step requires either upstream contribution or a project-side bridge plus \(\mathtt{IsReduced}\) on the intersection): produce the section \(\mathrm{(intersection)} \to \overline{\mathbb P^1}\) whose post- composition with \(\Delta \) equals \((\mathrm{chart}_0, \mathrm{chart}_1)\).

  4. The factorization reduces, by composing with the two projections \(\mathtt{prod.fst}\) and \(\mathtt{prod.snd}\) out of the codomain \(\overline{\mathbb P^1} \times _{\bar k} \overline{\mathbb P^1}\), to two scheme-equalities on the components — each of which is a direct chart-bridge equality on \((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover})_i\)’s structure (i.e. the single-chart content, not the full cocycle), discharged using the same chart-ring iso 204 and \(\mathtt{pullbackSpecIso}\) bridge that 219 uses. The chart-\(1\) instance of the per-component equality threads through the named \(\mathrm{Proj.appIso}\) evaluation 208, which packages the basic-open ring-map identification \(\mathtt{isLocElem} \mapsto [X_0/X_1] \mapsto 1\) at the global-sections level so the prover can target it directly without re-deriving the \((\mathrm{Proj.appIso}).\mathrm{inv}\) image of \(\mathtt{isLocElem}\) inline (the iter-188 through iter-191 budget-wall step). The chart-\(0\) instance is the symmetric statement with roles of \(X_0, X_1\) interchanged.

Substrate hooks. The categorical substrate is present at the pinned Mathlib commit b80f227: \(\mathtt{IsSeparated.diagonal\_ isClosedImmersion}\), \(\mathtt{IsClosedImmersion.lift}\) (kernel-inequality form), \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Limits.prod.lift}\), \(\mathtt{pullback.lift}\), together with the standard \(\mathtt{IsSeparated}\) instance for \(\operatorname {Proj}\) of a graded ring (Stacks 01KU / Hartshorne II.4.6 diagonal-is-closed characterisation). Cost estimate (post iter-188 falsification). \(\sim 150\)–\(200\) LOC over 3–5 iters (Option B: project-side substrate including \(\mathtt{lift\_ iff\_ range\_ subset}\) plus \(\mathtt{IsReduced}\) on the intersection); Option A (Mathlib upstream) is unbounded in time.

(IV) Substantive ring-level residual. All three pickup paths reduce, after their structural setup, to the same ring-level identity. On the basic-open intersection \(D_+(X_0 \cdot X_1) \subseteq \overline{\mathbb P^1}\) both chart coordinates are units (the chart-\(0\) coordinate \(t = X_1/X_0\) and the chart-\(1\) coordinate \(u = X_0/X_1\) are reciprocals: \(t \cdot u = 1\)), and the agreement reduces to the ring-level identity

\[ \lambda \cdot u \ =\ \frac{1}{t} \cdot \lambda \quad \text{in } \mathrm{Localization.Away}\, t \otimes _{\bar k} \mathtt{GmRing}\, \bar k, \]

i.e. to the unit-of-the-localisation identity \(t \cdot u = 1\) (\(\mathtt{IsLocalization.Away.mul\_ invSelf}\)) together with commutativity of the tensor product. The proof routes through the chart-ring iso 204 and the same \(\mathtt{pullbackSpecIso}\) bridge that 219 uses; consequently 220 remains gated on 219 acquiring its concrete body and on one of the (III.a)–(III.c) structural setups landing. See \(\mathtt{analogies/gmscaling{\text-}projection{\text-}idiom.md}\) (iter-184) for the full Recipe 1/2/3 decomposition and empirical simp-probe verification.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 291Cross-chart agreement of \(\sigma _\times \) (cocycle)

The substantive \((0,1)\) cross case of the \(\sigma _\times \) gluing condition: on the overlap \(D_+(X_0 X_1) \times \mathbb G_m\), the chart-\(0\) map (\(t \mapsto t \otimes \lambda ^{-1}\)) and the chart-\(1\) map (\(u \mapsto u \otimes \lambda \)) agree, i.e.

\[ \mathtt{pullback.fst} \mathbin {\, ;\, }(\mathtt{chart}\, 0) = \mathtt{pullback.snd} \mathbin {\, ;\, }(\mathtt{chart}\, 1). \]

This is the cocycle identity feeding 220.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 221

[Per-chart bridge \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ chart}\, i\, \mathbin {\, ;\, }\, \mathbb P^1.\mathrm{hom}\)]

For each chart index \(i \in \{ 0, 1\} = \mathrm{Fin}\, 2\), the chart-\(i\) scheme morphism \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ chart}\, \bar k\, i\) (219) intertwines the structure morphism of the cover chart \((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover}\, \bar k).f\, i\) (218) with the structure morphism of \(\mathbb P^1\):

\[ \mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ chart}\, \bar k\, i \, \mathbin {\, ;\, }\, \mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\, \bar k.\mathrm{hom} \ = (\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover}\, \bar k).f\, i \, \mathbin {\, ;\, }\, \bigl((\mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\, \bar k) \otimes \mathtt{Gm}\, \bar k\bigr).\mathrm{hom}. \]

This is the per-chart certificate that, after \(\mathtt{Scheme.Cover.hom\_ ext}\) followed by \(\mathtt{ι\_ glueMorphisms\_ assoc}\), closes the over-side coherence \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ over\_ coherence}\) (222) by which the glued total morphism \(\sigma _\times = \mathtt{gmScalingP1}\) (215) is promoted from a bare \(\mathbf{Sch}\)-level map to a morphism in \(\mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}\bar k)\). Three-step reduction. The proof has three steps. All three route through the project-side chart-bridge \(\mathtt{awayι\_ comp\_ PLB\_ hom}\) (\(\mathtt{Genus0BaseObjects.lean}\), post-iter-175 split moves to \(\mathtt{Genus0BaseObjects/ChartBridge.lean}\)), which identifies \(\mathrm{Proj.awayι} \, \mathbin {\, ;\, }\, \mathbb P^1.\mathrm{hom}\) with \(\operatorname{Spec}.\mathrm{map}\, (\mathrm{algebraMap}\, \bar k\, (\mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, X_i))\) for the degree-\(1\) homogeneous generators \(X_i\).

(A) Collapse \(\mathrm{Proj.awayι} \mathbin {\, ;\, }\mathbb P^1.\mathrm{hom}\).

Apply the chart-bridge \(\mathtt{awayι\_ comp\_ PLB\_ hom}\) to rewrite the tail of the chart-\(i\) morphism, replacing \(\mathrm{Proj.awayι} \, \mathbin {\, ;\, }\, \mathbb P^1.\mathrm{hom}\) with \(\operatorname{Spec}.\mathrm{map}\, (\mathrm{algebraMap}\, \bar k\, (\mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, X_i))\).

(B) Merge the \(\operatorname{Spec}.\mathrm{map}\)’s.

Use \(\operatorname{Spec}.\mathrm{map\_ comp}\) to merge the two consecutive \(\operatorname{Spec}.\mathrm{map}\)’s on the chart-\(i\) side, then apply the chart-ring iso’s \(\bar k\)-algebra preservation (207) together with \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial.eval}_2\mathrm{Hom}\_ C\) to identify the merged ring map with \(\mathrm{algebraMap}\, \bar k\, (\mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, X_i \otimes _{\bar k} \mathtt{GmRing}\, \bar k)\).

(C) Chase the source-side pullback isos.

Unfold the source-side iso \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover\_ X\_ iso}\, \bar k\, i\) (identifying the cover chart with \(\operatorname{Spec}\) of an \(\bar k\)-tensor product of chart-\(i\) and \(\mathtt{GmRing}\)). Then use Mathlib’s \(\mathtt{pullbackSpecIso\_ hom\_ base}\), \(\mathtt{pullbackRightPullbackFstIso\_ hom\_ fst}\), and \(\mathtt{pullbackSymmetry\_ hom\_ comp\_ fst}\) to chase the source side back to \((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover}\, \bar k).f\, i\) composed with the structure morphism of the relative tensor product. The final identification with the RHS is via Mathlib’s \(\mathtt{Over.tensorObj\_ hom}\) and \(\mathtt{pullback.condition}\).

Status (axiom-clean since iter-180; updated iter-185 review). Steps (A) and (B) are axiom-clean as of iter-174. Step (C) was closed kernel-clean \(\{ \mathtt{propext}, \mathtt{Classical.choice}, \mathtt{Quot.sound}\} \) in iter-180 via the \(\mathtt{set\_ option}\, \mathtt{backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency\, false}\) recipe (Decision-4 ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB from the pullbackspeciso-bypass analogist consult). The earlier syntactic \(\mathrm{Fin}\)-literal mismatch and the \(\mathtt{Algebra.compHom}\)-driven instance synthesis chain were both retired by that recipe; both iter-177 TEMP project axioms were dropped.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 223

[The \(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling action fixes the origin \(0 \in \mathbb P^1\)]

Let \(\sigma _\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1\) be the scaling action of 215. Then the composite

\[ \mathbb G_m \xrightarrow {\; (\mathrm{toUnit}_{\mathbb G_m} \mathbin {\, ;\, }0_{\mathbb P^1},\ \mathbf1_{\mathbb G_m})\; } \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \xrightarrow {\; \sigma _\times \; } \mathbb P^1 \]

equals the constant morphism \(\mathrm{toUnit}_{\mathbb G_m} \mathbin {\, ;\, }0_{\mathbb P^1}\). That is, \(\sigma _\times (0, \lambda ) = 0\) for every \(\lambda \in \mathbb G_m\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 222

[Over-structure coherence for the glued total morphism]

The total morphism obtained by \(\mathtt{Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms}\) from \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ chart}\, \bar k\) (219) and its cocycle \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ chart\_ agreement}\, \bar k\) (220) intertwines the structure morphisms to \(\operatorname{Spec}\bar k\):

\[ (\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover}\, \bar k).\mathtt{glueMorphisms}\, (\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ chart}\, \bar k)\, (\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ chart\_ agreement}\, \bar k) \mathbin {\, ;\, }\mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\, \bar k.\mathrm{hom} \ = ((\mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\, \bar k) \otimes \mathtt{Gm}\, \bar k).\mathrm{hom}. \]

This is the \(\mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}\bar k)\)-side commutation that promotes the bare scheme map on \((\mathbb P^1 \otimes \mathbb G_m).\mathrm{left}\) to a genuine morphism in \(\mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}\bar k)\), supplying the \(\mathtt{Over.homMk}\) second argument that the body of \(\sigma _\times = \mathtt{gmScalingP1}\) (215) consumes. Reduction. By \(\mathtt{Scheme.Cover.hom\_ ext}\) for the cover \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover}\) (218), the equation is equivalent to its chart-wise restrictions: for each \(i \in \mathrm{Fin}\, 2\), composing first with \((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover})_i.f \colon (\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ cover})_i \to (\mathbb P^1 \otimes \mathbb G_m).\mathrm{left}\) on the left, both sides land in \(\operatorname{Spec}\bar k\). Agreement there is automatic from the way \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_ chart}\, \bar k\, i\) is built: it factors through \(\operatorname{Spec}.\mathrm{map}\) of the \(\bar k\)-algebra map \(\mathrm{algebraMap}\, \bar k\, (\mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, X_i \otimes _{\bar k} \mathtt{GmRing})\), so its composition with the structure map down to \(\operatorname{Spec}\bar k\) matches the structure map of the cover chart. The argument is therefore mechanical given that 219 has a concrete body; like the cocycle, 222 is gated on

219. See \(\mathtt{analogies/chart{\text-}bridge.md}\) (iter-173 in flight)

for the chart-wise step.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 269\(\mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}\bar k)\) structure on \(\mathbb G_m\)

The natural \(\mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}\bar k)\) structure on \(\mathbb G_m\), with structure morphism \(\operatorname{Spec}\) of the algebra map \(\bar k \to \bar k[t, t^{-1}]\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 340

[(i.b-helper) Per-open IsIso of the inverse-direction morphism]

Let \(G : \mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}k)\) be as in 337, and let

\[ \texttt{basechange\_ along\_ proj\_ two\_ inv}\, G \; \colon \; (\texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\, \psi ).\mathrm{obj}\, \Omega _{G/k} \; \longrightarrow \; \Omega _{(G \times _{k} G)/G} \]

denote the inverse-direction morphism of 339 (with \(\psi \) the second-projection compatibility morphism of 335). Then for every open \(X \in (G \otimes G).\mathrm{left}.\mathrm{Opens}^{\mathrm{op}}\), the application

\[ (\texttt{basechange\_ along\_ proj\_ two\_ inv}\, G).\mathrm{app}\, X \; \; \colon \; \; \bigl((\texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\, \psi ).\mathrm{obj}\, \Omega _{G/k}\bigr).\mathrm{obj}\, X \; \longrightarrow \; \Omega _{(G \times _{k} G)/G}.\mathrm{obj}\, X \]

is an isomorphism of \(\Gamma ((G \otimes G).\mathrm{left},\, \mathrm{snd}^{-1}\, X)\)-modules.

Composed with the iso-reflection bridge isIso_of_app_iso_module (Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:544–550, closed iter-140; reflects pointwise iso-of-app to a presheaf-of-modules iso via PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf + NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app), this per-open conclusion supplies the third concrete sub-sorry of 337’s proof, completing the base-change iso of piece (i.b) Step 2.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 331(i.a-bridge) Bridge to the local-ring cotangent space at the identity

Let \(G\) be as in 329, and let \(\mathcal O_{G, \eta _G}\) denote the local ring of \(G\) at the identity (the stalk along \(\eta _G : \operatorname{Spec}k \to G\)), with maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m_{\eta _G}\) and residue map \(\pi \colon \mathcal O_{G, \eta _G} \to k\). Then there is a canonical \(k\)-linear isomorphism

\[ \mathfrak g^{\vee } \; =\; \eta _G^{*}\, \Omega _{G/k} \; \xrightarrow {\; \sim \; }\; \mathfrak m_{\eta _G} \big/ \mathfrak m_{\eta _G}^{2}. \]

Concretely, the right-hand side is Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace applied to the local ring \(\mathcal O_{G, \eta _G}\) (as a module over its residue field, identified with \(k\) via the \(\mathrm{GrpObj}\)-supplied identity section); the left-hand side is the iter-130+ chart-base-changed Kähler Lean body of 329.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 329

[(i.a) Cotangent space at the identity of a group scheme] The pullback along the identity section \(\eta _G\) of the relative cotangent presheaf,

\[ \mathfrak g^{\vee } \; :=\; \eta _G^{*}\, \Omega _{G/k}, \]

is a finitely generated free \(k\)-module. We call \(\mathfrak g^{\vee }\) the cotangent space of \(G\) at the identity. The Lie algebra of \(G\) as a \(k\)-vector space is recovered as the \(k\)-linear dual \(\mathfrak g := \mathfrak g^{\vee \vee } = \mathrm{Hom}_{k}(\mathfrak g^{\vee }, k)\) (the bracket structure is not used in the rigidity argument and is not packaged here).

Lean encoding note (iter-129 dualisation pinning). The Lean declaration AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity returns a ModuleCat k which is the un-dualised cotangent \(\mathfrak g^{\vee }\) — the direct output of the pullback-along-section construction described in the proof, with no internal Module.Dual hop. Downstream consumers that need the tangent \(\mathfrak g\) take Module.Dual k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G) externally. This convention pins what was left open in the iter-128 encoding note: the rigidity argument is symmetric under dualisation (only the finite-rank-free claim is consumed), so the un-dualised form is preferred as it matches the body construction without an extra contravariant hop. The declaration embeds into Mathlib’s ModuleCat infrastructure and supplies the Module.Free k / Module.Finite k typeclasses needed for the rank lemma 332 via the bridge lemma 331.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 330

[Structural-shape rewrite handle for the cotangent space at the identity]

Under the hypotheses of 329, there exist affine opens \(U \subseteq \operatorname{Spec}k\), \(V \subseteq G.\mathrm{left}\), an inclusion \(e \colon V \leq G.\mathrm{hom}^{-1}\, U\), and a top-inclusion \(h_{\mathrm{top}} \colon \top \leq \eta _{G}^{-1}\, V\) such that, under the \(\Gamma (\operatorname{Spec}k, U)\)-algebra structure on \(\Gamma (G, V)\) from appLE, the cotangent space at the identity equals the explicit chart-base-changed Kähler module:

\[ \mathfrak g^{\vee } \; =\; \texttt{cotangentSpaceAtIdentity}\, G \; =\; (\texttt{ModuleCat.extendScalars}\, \psi _{V}.\mathrm{hom}).\mathrm{obj}\bigl(\texttt{ModuleCat.of}\, \Gamma (G, V)\, \Omega [\Gamma (G, V)/\Gamma (\operatorname{Spec}k, U)]\bigr), \]

where \(\psi _{V} \colon \Gamma (G, V) \to k\) is the ring map obtained by restricting the identity section \(\eta _{G}\) to \(V\) and composing with the canonical iso \(\Gamma (\operatorname{Spec}k, \cdot ) \cong k\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 332

[(i.a) Rank of the cotangent space at the identity]

In the setting of 329, with \([\mathtt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}\, n\, G.\mathtt{hom}]\) supplying the relative dimension parameter,

\[ \operatorname{finrank}_{k}\, \mathfrak g^{\vee } \; =\; n, \]

and consequently (since rank is preserved under \(k\)-linear duality on finitely-generated free modules) \(\operatorname{finrank}_{k}\, \mathfrak g = n\) as well.

Lean encoding note (iter-128 per strategy-critic-iter128; iter-129 rename). The right-hand side is the relative-dimension parameter \(n\) from the [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom] instance, NOT a bare \(\dim G\) (which has no canonical Lean function on Over (Spec k)). The mathematical content is unchanged because for a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme, the relative dimension equals the dimension of the cotangent space at the identity equals what informal prose calls \(\dim G\). The Lean target name is cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq (iter-129 rename, replacing the iter-128 placeholder lieAlgebra_finrank_eq_dim, aligned with the renamed (i.a) declaration 329).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 334(i.b) Shear-iso globalisation of the cotangent

Let \(\mu \colon G \times _{k} G \to G\) denote the multiplication of \(G\) and let \(\sigma \colon G \times _{k} G \to G \times _{k} G\) be the shear isomorphism

\[ \sigma \; =\; \langle \mathrm{pr}_{1},\, \mu \rangle , \qquad (a, b) \; \mapsto \; (a,\, a \cdot b), \]

built categorically from \(\mu \) and the binary-product projections in \(\mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}k)\). Then \(\sigma \) is an isomorphism over the first projection, and pullback along \(\sigma \) together with the canonical first-projection-cotangent identification \(\Omega _{(G \times _{k} G)/G} \cong \mathrm{pr}_{2}^{*}\, \Omega _{G/k}\) (337) yields a natural isomorphism of presheaves of modules on \(G\)

\[ \Omega _{G/k} \; \cong \; \pi _{G}^{*}\! \bigl(\eta _{G}^{*}\, \Omega _{G/k}\bigr) \qquad \text{on } G, \]

where \(\pi _{G} \colon G \to \operatorname{Spec}k\) is the structure map (in the standard category-theory notation for the binary product in \(\mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}k)\), this is the "first projection" of the singleton-product-with-base, sometimes written \(\mathrm{pr}_{1}\); we use \(\pi _{G}\) to avoid confusion with the binary-product first projection \(\mathrm{pr}_{1} \colon G \times _{k} G \to G\) appearing above). Informally: the relative cotangent of \(G\) is canonically isomorphic to the constant \(\mathcal O_{G}\)-module with fibre \(\eta _{G}^{*}\, \Omega _{G/k}\), trivialised along the structure map.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 337(i.b-helper) Base-change of differentials along the second projection

Let \(G : \mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}k)\) be as above, and consider the binary product \(G \times _{k} G\) in \(\mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}k)\), with first and second projections \(\mathrm{pr}_{1}, \mathrm{pr}_{2} \colon G \times _{k} G \to G\). View \(G \times _{k} G\) as a \(G\)-scheme via \(\mathrm{pr}_{1}\). Then there is a canonical natural isomorphism of presheaves of modules on \(G \times _{k} G\)

\[ \Omega _{(G \times _{k} G)/G} \; \cong \; \mathrm{pr}_{2}^{*}\, \Omega _{G/k}, \]

where the left-hand side is the relative cotangent of \(G \times _{k} G\) viewed as a \(G\)-scheme via \(\mathrm{pr}_{1}\), and the right-hand side is the pullback along \(\mathrm{pr}_{2}\) of \(\Omega _{G/k} = \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}\, G.\mathrm{hom}\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 339(i.b-helper) Inverse-direction morphism for the base-change iso

With \(G, \psi \) as in 338, the universal property of \(\Omega _{G/k} = \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}\, G.\mathrm{hom}\) (Mathlib’s PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal’ at Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Differentials/Presheaf.lean:189) applied to the derivation \(D\) of 338, then transposed along the PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction \(\psi \) (at Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Pullback.lean:50), produces an inverse morphism

\[ \texttt{basechange\_ along\_ proj\_ two\_ inv}\, G \; \colon \; (\texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\, \psi ).\mathrm{obj}\, \Omega _{G/k} \; \longrightarrow \; \Omega _{(G \times _{k} G)/G}. \]

This morphism is the inverse of the iso of 337; the iso property \(\texttt{IsIso}\, (\texttt{basechange\_ along\_ proj\_ two\_ inv}\, G)\) is the third concrete sub-sorry of 337’s proof block (iter-140 prover-lane target, Route (b’2) per analogies/isiso-basechange-along-proj-two-inv.md — see the Route (b’2) sub-paragraph of the IsIso NOTE block in that proof).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 338(i.b-helper) Inverse-direction derivation for the base-change iso

Let \(G : \mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}k)\) be as in 337, and let

\[ \psi \; :=\; (\mathrm{Scheme}.\operatorname{Hom}.\texttt{toRingCatSheafHom}\, (\texttt{snd}\, G\, G).\mathrm{left}).\hom \]

be the compatibility morphism for the second projection \(\mathrm{pr}_2 = \texttt{snd}\, G\, G : G \otimes G \to G\) (the iter-135 Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom idiom, in the shape consumed by PresheafOfModules.pullback; see 336). There is a \(\varphi _G\)-derivation

\[ D \; \colon \; \bigl((\texttt{PresheafOfModules.pushforward}\, \psi ).\mathrm{obj}\, \Omega _{(G \times _{k} G)/G}\bigr).\texttt{Derivation'}\, \varphi _G \]

on the transparent pushforward of the left-hand-side relative cotangent along \(\psi \), where \(\varphi _G\) is the \(\mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}k)\)-side compatibility morphism for \(G.\mathrm{hom}\) obtained via the homEquiv-transpose \(((\texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\, \mathrm{CommRingCat}\, G.\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{base}).\texttt{homEquiv}\, \_ \, \_ ).\texttt{symm}\, G.\mathrm{hom}.c\). The derivation \(D\) is defined pointwise at each open \(X \in G.\mathrm{left}.\mathrm{Opens}^{\mathrm{op}}\) by

\[ d_X(b) \; :=\; \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.D}\, \_ \, ((\psi .\mathrm{app}\, X).\hom \, b), \]

i.e. push the section \(b \in G.\mathrm{left}.\texttt{presheaf}.\mathrm{obj}\, X\) forward along \(\psi .\mathrm{app}\, X\) to a section of \((G \otimes G).\mathrm{left}.\texttt{presheaf}.\mathrm{obj}\, (\mathrm{snd}^{-1}\, X)\), then take its universal Kähler differential in the source-side presheaf of relative differentials of \((\texttt{fst}\, G\, G).\mathrm{left}\) over the pulled-back open.

The Lean construction uses PresheafOfModules.Derivation’.mk at the per-open level via ModuleCat.Derivation.mk with the rule above; the additive (d_add) and Leibniz (d_mul) laws follow from the RingHom-ness of \(\psi .\mathrm{app}\, X\) composed with the algebra-side derivation laws ModuleCat.Derivation.d_add / d_mul on KaehlerDifferential.D. The d_app (algebraic-coherence, zero on \(\varphi _G\)-image) and d_map (cross-open naturality) sub-goals remain sorry-bodied in iter-138; see the iter-138 NOTE block of 337’s proof above for the closure recipes.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 342(i.c) Freeness of the relative cotangent of \(G\)

The relative cotangent presheaf \(\Omega _{G/k}\) is a free \(\mathcal O_{G}\)-module.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 343(i.c) Rank of the relative cotangent of \(G\)

The (constant) rank of \(\Omega _{G/k}\) as a free \(\mathcal O_{G}\)-module equals \(n\), where \(n\) is the relative dimension supplied by \([\mathtt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}\, n\, G.\mathtt{hom}]\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 341(i.b-helper) Cotangent at the identity via restriction along the canonical section

Let \(s := \langle \mathrm{id}_{G},\, \eta _{G} \rangle \colon G \to G \times _{k} G\) denote the canonical section of the first projection \(\mathrm{pr}_{1}\) in \(\mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}k)\), and let \(\pi _{G} \colon G \to \operatorname{Spec}k\) denote the structure map. The categorical identity \(\mathrm{pr}_{2} \circ s = \eta _{G} \circ \pi _{G}\) in \(\mathrm{Over}\, (\operatorname{Spec}k)\) — which holds by direct evaluation of the binary-product second projection on the \(\langle \mathrm{id}_{G}, \eta _{G} \rangle \) pair — yields a canonical natural isomorphism of presheaves of modules on \(G\):

\[ s^{*}\! \bigl(\mathrm{pr}_{2}^{*}\, \Omega _{G/k}\bigr) \; \cong \; \pi _{G}^{*}\! \bigl(\eta _{G}^{*}\, \Omega _{G/k}\bigr). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 333

[(i.b-helper) Binary-product shear isomorphism for a group object]

Let \(G\) be a group object in a Cartesian monoidal category. The binary-product shear isomorphism is the self-iso

\[ \sigma \colon G \otimes G \; \xrightarrow {\; \sim \; }\; G \otimes G \]

whose forward and inverse maps are built categorically from the multiplication \(\mu \), the inverse \(\iota \), and the binary-product projections of \(G \otimes G\):

\[ \sigma _{\mathrm{hom}} \; =\; \texttt{lift\, (fst\, G\, G)\, μ}, \qquad \sigma _{\mathrm{inv}} \; =\; \texttt{lift\, (fst\, G\, G)\, (lift\, (fst\, G\, G\, ≫\, $\iota $)\, (snd\, G\, G)\, ≫\, μ)}, \]

informally \(\sigma \colon (a, b) \mapsto (a, a \cdot b)\) with inverse \((a, b) \mapsto (a, a^{-1} \cdot b)\). The forward map is an isomorphism over the first projection and acts as right-translation on the second coordinate; concretely,

\[ \sigma _{\mathrm{hom}} \circ \mathrm{pr}_{1} \; =\; \mathrm{pr}_{1}, \qquad \sigma _{\mathrm{hom}} \circ \mathrm{pr}_{2} \; =\; \mu . \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 849

[H\(^0\) of a closed-point skyscraper sheaf has dimension 1] Iter-188 Lane H named substrate. Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over \(\bar k\) and \(P \in C\) a closed point. Then

\[ \dim _{\bar k}\, H^0(C, \mathtt{skyscraperSheaf}\, P\, (\mathrm{Module}.\mathtt{of}\, \bar k\, \bar k)) \; =\; 1. \]

Closed axiom-clean iter-188 via the three-step \(\bar k\)-linear- equivalence chain HModule_zero_linearEquiv \(\to \) constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv \(\to \) SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top, followed by LinearEquiv.finrank_eq and Module.finrank_self on the dite-resolved skyscraper presheaf evaluation at \(\top \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 850

[H\(^1\) of a closed-point skyscraper sheaf vanishes] Iter-188 Lane H named substrate (typed-sorry blocker; committed RR.2.H\(^1\) STRATEGY sub-phase). Let \(C\) be as above. Then

\[ \dim _{\bar k}\, H^1(C, \mathtt{skyscraperSheaf}\, P\, (\mathrm{Module}.\mathtt{of}\, \bar k\, \bar k)) \; =\; 0. \]

Mathematically: the closed-point skyscraper sheaf is flasque (any pushforward from a one-point space is flasque; Hartshorne III.2.5(c)), and flasque sheaves have vanishing higher cohomology on any topological space (Hartshorne III.2.5). Gated on either (a) upstream Mathlib Sheaf.flasque_vanishing_cohomology at the Ext-cohomology level for sheaves valued in an abelian category with enough injectives, or (b) a project-side flasque-cohomology bridge ( 150-300 LOC; the committed 35-style sub-build).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 870

[\(H^1\) of a closed-point skyscraper sheaf has dimension zero]

Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over \(\bar k\) and let \(P \in C\) be a closed point. Then

\[ \dim _{\bar k}\, H^1\bigl(C,\, \mathtt{skyscraperSheaf}\; P\; (\mathrm{Module}.\mathtt{of}\; \bar k\; \bar k)\bigr) \; =\; 0, \]

where the left-hand side is the \(\bar k\)-finrank of \(\operatorname{HModule}\; \bar k\; (\mathtt{skyscraperSheaf}\; P\; (\mathrm{Module}.\mathtt{of}\; \bar k\; \bar k))\; 1\).

Project-bespoke assembly of the two substrate inputs of this chapter (865 and 869). The chapter’s external source is Hartshorne III.2 Proposition 2.5 applied to the flasque skyscraper sheaf — both inputs are quoted verbatim in their own lemma blocks above.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 899

[Vanishing of \(H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(P))\) on a genus-\(0\) curve]

Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, p. 296, the inductive step of the proof of Theorem 1.3. Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over \(\bar k\) with \(g(C) = 0\), and let \(P \in C\) be a closed point. Then

\[ H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) \; =\; 0, \]

i.e. \(\dim _{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 732

[Syzygy descent of \(\texttt{HasProjectiveDimensionLT}\)] Source: Mathlib’s \(\texttt{ShortComplex.ShortExact.hasProjectiveDimensionLT\_ X\_ 1}\) + \(\texttt{ModuleCat.projective\_ of\_ free}\). Let \(f : R^n \twoheadrightarrow M\) be a surjection of \(R\)-modules with \(\texttt{HasProjectiveDimensionLT}\, (\texttt{ModuleCat.of}\, R\, M)\, (k+2)\). Then \(\texttt{HasProjectiveDimensionLT}\, (\texttt{ModuleCat.of}\, R\, (\ker f))\, (k+1)\). Proof: build the SES \(0 \to \ker f \to R^n \to M \to 0\) via \(\texttt{LinearMap.shortExact\_ shortComplexKer}\); discharge projectivity of \(R^n\) via \(\texttt{ModuleCat.projective\_ of\_ free} (\texttt{Pi.basisFun}\, R\, (\texttt{Fin}\, n))\); lift \(\texttt{HasProjectiveDimensionLT}\, 1\) on \(R^n\) up to \(k+1\) via \(\texttt{hasProjectiveDimensionLT\_ of\_ ge}\); apply \(\texttt{ShortExact.hasProjectiveDimensionLT\_ X\_ 1}\) to close.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 733

[Ascent companion: \(\ker f\)-pd-control \(\Rightarrow M\)-pd-control] Companion ascent direction: with the same SES, \(\texttt{HasProjectiveDimensionLT}\, (\ker f)\, (k+1)\) gives \(\texttt{HasProjectiveDimensionLT}\, M\, (k+2)\) via \(\texttt{ShortExact.hasProjectiveDimensionLT\_ X\_ 3}\). Together with 732 this enables the contradiction argument that pins \(\texttt{pd}\, \ker f = k\) exactly (rather than merely \(\leq k\)) under \(\texttt{pd}\, M = k+1\) exactly.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 731

[Bridge: \(\texttt{projectiveDimension}\, M = n \Rightarrow \texttt{HasProjectiveDimensionLT}\, M\, (n+1)\)] Source: Mathlib’s \(\texttt{CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension\_ lt\_ iff}\). For a Noetherian local ring \(R\) and a finite \(R\)-module \(M\) with \(\texttt{Module.projectiveDimension}\, R\, M = ((n : \mathbb N) : \texttt{WithBot}\, \mathbb N_\infty )\), one has \(\texttt{HasProjectiveDimensionLT}\, (\texttt{ModuleCat.of}\, R\, M)\, (n+1)\). Single-rewrite proof via Mathlib’s \(\texttt{projectiveDimension\_ lt\_ iff}\). The role: bridge from the AB chain’s existing pd-equality input form to the SES-descent predicate consumed by the iter-200 helpers below.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 144

[Relative affine vanishing] Source: Stacks Project, Tag 02KG (“Relative affine vanishing”). Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. If \(f\) is affine, then \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F} = 0\) for all \(i \geq 1\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 143

[Quasi-coherence of higher direct images] Source: Stacks Project, Tag 02KE (“Quasi-coherence of higher direct images”), part (1). Let \(f : X \to S\) be a quasi-compact and quasi-separated morphism of schemes. Then for every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\) and every \(p \geq 0\), the higher direct image \(R^p f_*\mathcal{F}\) is a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 256Right round-trip of the chart-ring iso

The composite \(\text{forward} \circ \text{inverse}\) is the identity on \(\bar k[u]\); i.e. 253 is a left inverse of 255.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 875Subsingleton carrier for flasque vanishing

For a flasque sheaf \(\mathcal F\) of \(\bar k\)-modules on \(X\) and any \(n\), the cohomology \(\operatorname{HModule}\; \bar k\; \mathcal F\; (n+1)\) is subsingleton. This is the strong-induction-on-\(n\) carrier of 865: the base case applies the degree-one collapse, and the inductive step reduces to the flasque quotient sheaf at one lower degree.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 874Injective sheaves have vanishing higher cohomology

For an injective object \(\mathcal I\) of the \(\bar k\)-module sheaf category on \(X\) and any \(i \ge 1\), the derived global-sections cohomology \(\operatorname{HModule}\; \bar k\; \mathcal I\; i\) has \(\bar k\)-finrank zero. This supplies the \(\operatorname{Ext}^i(-, \mathcal I) = 0\) input of the long-exact-sequence chase.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 188

[Additive decomposition over a product (Milne Corollary 1.5)]

Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Corollary 1.5. Let \(V\), \(W\) be complete varieties over \(\bar k\) with \(\bar k\)-points \(v_0 \in V\), \(w_0 \in W\), and let \(p \colon V \times W \to V\), \(q \colon V \times W \to W\) be the projections. Let \(A\) be an abelian variety over \(\bar k\). Then a morphism \(h \colon V \times W \to A\) with \(h(v_0, w_0) = 0\) can be written uniquely as \(h = f \circ p + g \circ q\), with morphisms \(f \colon V \to A\), \(g \colon W \to A\) satisfying \(f(v_0) = 0\) and \(g(w_0) = 0\). Lean encoding. In the project \(A\) is a group object [GrpObj A] (not assumed commutative). The “\(+\)” here is the binary operation that GrpObj induces on each hom-set \(\mathrm{Hom}(X, A)\): for \(u, v \colon X \to A\), \(u + v\) is the composite \(X \xrightarrow {\langle u, v\rangle } A \times A \xrightarrow {\; \mathrm{mul}\; } A\) (and \(f \circ p + g \circ q\) is built this way from the projections). The decomposition is therefore read multiplicatively as \(h = (f \circ p) \cdot (g \circ q)\) in the group \(\mathrm{Hom}(V \times W, A)\), and does not require \(A\) commutative; commutativity of abelian varieties (Milne Cor 1.4, a separate consequence of 189) is needed only to rewrite the product symmetrically/additively, not for the statement or its proof. The prover should infer the hom-set group operation from the GrpObj instance, not from a CommGrpObj instance.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 521Component agreement up to heterogeneous equality

The component of a morphism of presheaves of modules at two equal objects agrees up to heterogeneous equality.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 230A pointed \(\mathbb P^1 \to A\) is constant (Milne Prop 3.9, via the 4th route)

Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Proposition 3.9. Off the genus-\(0\) critical path (iter-164): retained alternative route. This \(\mathbb G_a\)-additive close of the base case is superseded by the \(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling shortcut now installed as the proof of 231 (see 229). It is retained as the classical Milne Proposition 3.9 argument (faithful to the \(\mathbb G_a \to A\) homomorphism / \(\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0\) presentation), but the live consumer 231 no longer depends on it; it is provided here only for completeness. Let \(A\) be an abelian variety over \(\bar k\) and \(f \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A\) a morphism with \(f(0) = 0_A\) (the additive-identity \(\bar k\)-point of \(\mathbb G_a = \mathbb A^1 \subset \mathbb P^1\); any base point reduces to this by a normalising translation in \(A\)). Then \(f\) is constant. Concretely: the translation action \(\sigma \) (215) makes \(f|_{\mathbb G_a}\) an additive-group homomorphism \(\mathbb G_a \to A\), and a homomorphism \(\mathbb G_a \to A\) is trivial (228), so \(f\) collapses to the constant \(0_A\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 228\(\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0\): a homomorphism from the additive group into an abelian variety is trivial

Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Proposition 3.9. Off the genus-\(0\) critical path (iter-164). This \(\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0\) lemma is superseded by the \(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling shortcut (231, 229), which closes the base case without it. It is retained only as the classical Milne Proposition 3.9 (\(\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m\) incompatibility) argument feeding the alternative route 230; no block on the genus-\(0\) critical path depends on it. Let \(A\) be an abelian variety over the algebraically closed field \(\bar k\), and let \(\varphi \colon \mathbb G_a \to A\) be a homomorphism of group objects (so \(\varphi (0) = 0_A\) and \(\varphi (x + y) = \varphi (x) + \varphi (y)\)). Then \(\varphi \) is the trivial homomorphism: it factors through the terminal object as the constant morphism at the identity, \(\varphi = \mathtt{toUnit}\, \mathbb G_a \mathbin {\, ;\, }\eta _A\). Equivalent completeness phrasing. Equivalently: a homomorphism from an affine connected group object into a complete group object is trivial. That is the abstract content; the proof below realises it characteristic-free.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 739Ideal action on a constant product

For a commutative ring \(R\), an ideal \(I\), a finite index type \(\iota \), and an \(R\)-module \(M\), the submodule \(I \cdot \top _{\iota \to M}\) equals the product submodule \(\prod _{\iota } (I \cdot \top _M)\) of fibrewise ideal actions.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 740Triviality of the ideal action on a constant product

For a nonempty finite index type \(\iota \): \(I \cdot \top _{\iota \to M} = \top \) if and only if \(I \cdot \top _M = \top \). The forward direction reads off the fibre witness from a \(\text{Pi.single}\) projection; the backward direction lifts a fibre witness through \(\text{Pi.single}\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 656The carrier of the identity component is connected

Let \(k\) be a field and \(G\) a \(k\)-group scheme locally of finite type. The carrier of the identity component (655), with its subspace topology, is a connected topological space.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 657The identity component is connected

Let \(k\) be a field and \(G\) a \(k\)-group scheme locally of finite type. The underlying topological space of the identity component \(G^0\) (645) is connected.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 661The identity component is geometrically connected

Let \(k\) be a field and \(G\) a \(k\)-group scheme locally of finite type. The structural morphism \(G^0 \to \operatorname{Spec}k\) of the identity component is geometrically connected.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 653The underlying space of a locally-finite-type \(k\)-scheme is locally connected

Let \(k\) be a field and \(G\) an object of the over-category over \(\operatorname{Spec}k\) whose structural morphism \(G \to \operatorname{Spec}k\) is locally of finite type. Then the underlying topological space \(|G|\) is locally connected.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 660The lifted identity section is a section

Let \(k\) be a field and \(G\) a \(k\)-group scheme locally of finite type. The morphism \(\operatorname{Spec}k \to G^0\) of 659 is a section of the structural morphism \(G^0 \to \operatorname{Spec}k\): composing it with \(G^0 \to \operatorname{Spec}k\) recovers the identity on \(\operatorname{Spec}k\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 658The identity section lands in the identity-component carrier

Let \(k\) be a field and \(G\) a \(k\)-group scheme locally of finite type. The image of the identity section \(e : \operatorname{Spec}k \to G\) is contained in the carrier of the identity component (655).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 559Reindexed open recovers its left component

For an object \(V \in \mathtt{Over}\, U\), the open-immersion image of the reindexed open \(e.\mathtt{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\, V = \iota ^{-1}(V.\mathrm{left})\) is \(V.\mathrm{left}\) itself,

\[ \iota _{\ast }\, \bigl(e.\mathtt{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\, V\bigr) \; =\; V.\mathrm{left}, \]

because \(V.\mathrm{left} \le U\). This is the equality of opens that identifies the two structure-sheaf presheaves underlying the ring morphism \(\varphi \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 553Locally injective implies injective on stalks

The converse: a locally injective morphism of presheaves over a topological space induces injective stalk maps.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 872Injective-embedding short complex is short exact

The short complex 871 is short exact: the embedding is a monomorphism, the cokernel projection is an epimorphism, and the sequence is exact at the middle term.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 494

[Evaluation/contraction of the internal hom] Source: [Stacks Project], “Modules on Ringed Spaces”, §Internal Hom (tag area 01CM). With \(R\) and \(M\) as above, there is a canonical evaluation (contraction) morphism of presheaves of \(R\)-modules

\[ \mathrm{ev}_M \; \colon \; M \otimes _R M^\vee \; \longrightarrow \; R, \qquad s \otimes \phi \longmapsto \phi (s), \]

the counit of the tensor–hom adjunction \(\mathcal{H}om(M \otimes _R -, R) \cong \mathcal{H}om(-, M^\vee )\) specialised at \(R\). On each open \(U\) it is the open-by-open contraction \((M|_U) \otimes _{R|_U} (M|_U \to R|_U) \to R|_U\), \(s \otimes \phi \mapsto \phi (s)\), and these are compatible with restriction, so they assemble to a morphism of presheaves of modules. The tensor product is the presheaf tensor underlying 403.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 535Value of the contraction on a simple tensor

The contraction sends \(s \otimes \varphi \) to \(\mathtt{evalLin}\, \varphi \, s\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 495

[The internal hom is a sheaf; the sheaf-level dual] Source: [Stacks Project], “Modules on Ringed Spaces”, §Internal Hom (tag area 01CM). Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(R = X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\), and let \(M, N \in \mathrm{Scheme}.\mathtt{Modules}\, X\) with \(N\) a sheaf of modules. Then the presheaf internal hom \(\mathcal{H}om(M, N)\) of 491 satisfies the sheaf condition: for an open cover \(\{ U_i\} \) of \(U\), a family of morphisms \(M|_{U_i} \to N|_{U_i}\) agreeing on the overlaps \(U_i \cap U_j\) glues to a unique morphism \(M|_U \to N|_U\), because \(N\) is a sheaf and morphisms of sheaves of modules are determined and gluable section-wise. Hence \(\mathcal{H}om(M, N)\) descends to an object of \(\mathrm{Scheme}.\mathtt{Modules}\, X\). Specialising to \(N = \mathcal{O}_X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\, R\) gives the sheaf-level dual

\[ \mathtt{dual}\, M \; :=\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(M, \mathcal{O}_X) \; \in \; \mathrm{Scheme}.\mathtt{Modules}\, X, \]

and the evaluation 494 descends to a morphism of sheaves of modules \(M \otimes _X \mathtt{dual}\, M \to \mathcal{O}_X\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 208

[Chart-\(1\) \(\mathrm{Proj.appIso}\) evaluation: \(\mathtt{isLocElem} \mapsto [X_0/X_1] \mapsto 1\)] Let \(\mathcal A = \bar k[X_0, X_1]\) be the standard \(\mathbb N\)-graded polynomial ring, and consider the homogeneous chart morphism at the degree-\(1\) generator \(X_1\),

\[ \mathrm{Proj.away}\iota \, X_1 \colon \operatorname{Spec}\, (\mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, X_1) \longrightarrow \operatorname {Proj}\, \mathcal A, \]

realising the basic open \(D_+(X_1) \subseteq \overline{\mathbb P^1} = \operatorname {Proj}\, \mathcal A\) as an affine open. Mathlib’s \(\mathtt{Proj.appIso}\) (applied to the open immersion \(\mathrm{Proj.away}\iota \, X_1\)) packages, at the level of global sections over \(\top \), the canonical scheme-side identification

\[ \Gamma (\operatorname{Spec}(\mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, X_1),\, \top ) \ =\ \mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, X_1 \ \xleftarrow {\ \sim \ } \Gamma (\operatorname {Proj}\, \mathcal A,\, D_+(X_1)) \ =\ \mathrm{HomogeneousLocalization.Away}\, \mathcal A\, X_1. \]

Under this identification, the canonical local-section element \(\mathtt{isLocElem} \in \Gamma (\operatorname{Spec}(\mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, X_1), \top )\) (the global section of \(\operatorname{Spec}\) corresponding under the affine Spec / global-sections adjunction to the identity ring map) is the image, via \((\mathtt{Proj.appIso}\, (\mathrm{Proj.away}\iota \, X_1)).\mathrm{inv}\), of the homogeneous fraction

\[ [X_0/X_1] \ \in \ \mathrm{HomogeneousLocalization.Away}\, \mathcal A\, X_1 \]

— the ratio of the two degree-\(1\) generators, i.e. the chart-\(1\) affine coordinate \(u = X_0/X_1\). Composing further with the chart-\(1\) ring identification \(\mathrm{forward}_1 \colon \mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, X_1 \xrightarrow {\sim } \mathrm{MvPolynomial}\, \{ *\} \, \bar k\) of 204 sends \([X_0/X_1]\) to the polynomial generator \(u\); then pre-composing with the source-side \(\mathtt{pullbackSpecIso}\) bridge of 219 (with the trivial \(\mathbb G_m\) factor at the identity section \(\lambda = 1\) collapsed by \(\mathtt{Algebra.TensorProduct.lid}\)) lands at \(1\) in the codomain ring. The combined composite ring map therefore sends \(\mathtt{isLocElem}\) to \(1 \in \bar k\):

\[ (\mathtt{iotaGm\_ chart1\_ composition}).\mathrm{appTop}\, (\mathtt{isLocElem}) \ =\ 1 \quad \text{in the codomain ring.} \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 300Chart-\(1\) composition is an open immersion

The composite of the chart-\(1\) section (299) with the chart-\(1\) scaling map (219) is an open immersion \(\mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1\). It realises the canonical inclusion \(\lambda \mapsto [\lambda : 1]\) as the three-step composite \(\operatorname{Spec}\bar k[t, t^{-1}] \hookrightarrow \operatorname{Spec}\bar k[u] \cong \operatorname{Spec}(\mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, X_1) \hookrightarrow \mathbb P^1\): the first map is a localisation open immersion, the middle is the chart-ring iso, and the last is the chart-\(1\) open immersion, with the composition identity supplied by 208.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 298Inner-lift compatibility for the chart-\(1\) section

The compatibility identity needed to build the chart-\(1\) section as a pullback lift: the two legs \((\iota _{\mathbb G_m} \mathbin {\, ;\, }[1:1]) \mathbin {\, ;\, }\mathbb P^1.\mathrm{hom}\) and \(\mathbf1 \mathbin {\, ;\, }\mathbb G_m.\mathrm{hom}\) agree, which reduces to the structure-map compatibility \(\mathtt{Over.w}\) of the point \([1:1]\) (195) over the base.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 303The canonical inclusion \(\mathbb G_m \hookrightarrow \mathbb P^1\) is dominant

The canonical inclusion \(\iota \colon \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1\) is dominant: its image is dense. Its image is a non-empty open (302) of the irreducible \(\mathbb P^1\) (geometric irreducibility, 201, over the one-point base \(\operatorname{Spec}\bar k\)), and a non-empty open of an irreducible space is dense.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 301The canonical inclusion \(\mathbb G_m \hookrightarrow \mathbb P^1\) is an open immersion

The canonical morphism \(\iota \colon \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1\) (the “specialise the scaling action at \(x = 1\)” map) is an open immersion. After unfolding it as a pullback lift glued through the scaling cover, it agrees with the chart-\(1\) composite of 300 (via 299 and 298), which is an open immersion.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 297The chart-\(1\) lift is the Spec of the evaluation map

The chart-\(1\) lift \(r_1\) (293) coincides with the Spec of the chart-\(1\) evaluation ring map (295): \(r_1 = \operatorname{Spec}(\mathtt{kbarChart1Ring})\). Both satisfy the same lift factorisation through \(\mathrm{Proj.away}\iota \, X_1\) (294, 296), so they agree by uniqueness of the open-immersion lift.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 294Factorisation of \([1:1]\) through chart \(1\)

The chart-\(1\) lift \(r_1\) (293) factors the point \([1:1]\) through the chart-\(1\) open immersion: \(r_1 \mathbin {\, ;\, }\mathrm{Proj.away}\iota \, X_1 = [1:1]\). Immediate from the defining factorisation property of the open-immersion lift.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 292Chart-\(1\) range containment of the point \([1:1]\)

The topological image of the \(\bar k\)-point \([1:1] \in \mathbb P^1\) (195) lies inside the open image of the chart-\(1\) affine open \(\mathrm{Proj.away}\iota \, X_1 \colon \operatorname{Spec}(\mathrm{Away}\, \mathcal A\, X_1) \to \operatorname {Proj}\, \mathcal A\), i.e. inside the basic open \(D_+(X_1)\). Indeed \([1:1]\) is the global-sections point with \(X_0, X_1 \mapsto 1\), so the value of \(X_1\) is the unit \(1\), and the preimage of \(D_+(X_1)\) under this point is all of \(\operatorname{Spec}\bar k\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 302The image of \(\mathbb G_m \hookrightarrow \mathbb P^1\) is open

The topological image of the canonical inclusion \(\iota \colon \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1\) is open in \(\mathbb P^1\). Immediate from \(\iota \) being an open immersion (301).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 552Injective on stalks implies locally injective

For a morphism of presheaves valued in a concrete category over a topological space, stalkwise injectivity implies local injectivity for the topological Grothendieck topology.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 546Local injectivity is preserved by flat left-whiskering

For sectionwise-flat \(F\), if \(g\) is locally injective then so is \(F \triangleleft g\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 545Local surjectivity is preserved by left-whiskering

If \(g\) is locally surjective then so is \(F \triangleleft g\), with no flatness hypothesis (pure right-exactness of \(\otimes \)).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 307

[Pointed condition for the universal morphism]

The universal morphism \(h.\mathtt{ofCurve}\) sends the marked point \(P\) to the identity of \(J\):

\[ P \circ h.\mathtt{ofCurve} \; =\; \eta _J. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 308

[Universal factorisation through the Albanese object]

For every morphism \(f \colon C \to A\) from \(C\) to a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme \(A\) over \(k\) with \(P \circ f = \eta _A\), there exists a unique morphism \(g \colon J \to A\) such that \(f = h.\mathtt{ofCurve} \circ g\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 673Indeterminacy locus is closed

For any rational map \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) of schemes, the indeterminacy locus \(Z(f) = X \smallsetminus \mathrm{Dom}(f)\) (672) is a closed subset of \(X\): it is the complement of the open domain of definition \(\mathrm{Dom}(f)\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 289Helper: \(\bar k[u] \otimes _{\bar k} K\) is a domain

For any field \(K\) over \(\bar k\), the ring \(\bar k[u] \otimes _{\bar k} K\) is an integral domain (the “geometrically irreducible affine line” tensor identity).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 757Zero cotangent dimension forces a field

For a Noetherian local ring \(R\) with \(\text{spanFinrank}(\mathfrak m) = 0\), the maximal ideal collapses to \(0\), so \(R\) is a field and hence an integral domain. This is the base case of 761.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 761Regular local rings are domains

Every regular Noetherian local ring \(R\) is an integral domain. The proof is a strong induction on \(\text{spanFinrank}(\mathfrak m)\): the base case is the field case (757); the inductive step picks \(x \in \mathfrak m \setminus \mathfrak m^2\) (756), uses that \(R/(x)\) is regular local of smaller dimension (758) and a domain by induction, and rules out the obstruction case via 760.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 861

[Constant sheaf on an irreducible space is flasque] Source: Hartshorne, II.1, Exercise 1.16(a). Let \(X\) be an irreducible topological space and let \(A\) be a \(\bar k\)-module. Then the constant sheaf \((\mathrm{constantSheaf}\; J\; \_ )\; A\) on \(X\) is flasque.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 864

[Injective objects of \(\mathrm{Sheaf}(X, \mathrm{Module}\bar k)\) are flasque] Source: Hartshorne, III.2, Lemma 2.4.

Let \(I\) be an injective object of \(\mathrm{Sheaf}\; (\mathrm{Opens}.\mathtt{grothendieckTopology}\; X)\; (\mathrm{Module}\bar k)\). Then \(I\) is flasque in the sense of 859: for every inclusion \(V \subseteq U\) of opens of \(X\), the restriction map \(I(U) \to I(V)\) is surjective on underlying \(\bar k\)-modules.

The classical argument (Hartshorne III Lemma 2.4) uses the extension-by-zero \(j_!\) functor: for an open inclusion \(j_V \colon V \hookrightarrow X\), one has the short exact sequence \(0 \to j_{V!}\mathcal O_V \to j_{U!}\mathcal O_U \to j_{U!}\mathcal O_U / j_{V!}\mathcal O_V \to 0\), and injectivity of \(I\) makes the induced \(\mathrm{Hom}(j_{U!}\mathcal O_U, I) \to \mathrm{Hom}(j_{V!}\mathcal O_V, I)\) surjective. Translating through the \(j_{(-)!} \dashv j_{(-)}^*\) adjunction (or the equivalent presheaf form for the constant ring sheaf \(\bar k\)) identifies the two Hom-sets with \(I(U)\) and \(I(V)\) respectively, delivering the surjective restriction map.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 860

[Pushforward of a flasque sheaf is flasque] Source: Hartshorne, II.1, Exercise 1.16(d). Let \(f \colon X \to Y\) be a continuous map of topological spaces and let \(\mathcal F\) be a flasque sheaf on \(X\). Then the pushforward sheaf \(f_*\mathcal F\) is flasque on \(Y\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 880Sections-surjectivity from a flasque leftmost term, Hartshorne II.1 Ex. 1.16(b)

For a short exact sequence \(0 \to S_1 \to S_2 \to S_3 \to 0\) of sheaves of \(\bar k\)-modules on \(X\) whose leftmost term \(S_1\) is flasque, the section-level map \(S_2(U) \to S_3(U)\) is surjective for every open \(U \subseteq X\). This is the Hartshorne II.1 Exercise 1.16(b) input consumed inside the flasque-vanishing argument.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 879Project flasqueness transfers to Mathlib flasqueness via \(\mathrm{forget}_2\)

Applying \(\mathrm{sheafCompose}\) along the forgetful functor \(\mathrm{Module}\; \bar k \to \mathrm{AddCommGrp}\) to a flasque sheaf \(\mathcal F\) (in the sense of 859) yields an \(\mathrm{AddCommGrp}\)-valued sheaf that is flasque in Mathlib’s sense: sectionwise surjectivity of restriction maps becomes epimorphy. The underlying section-level functions are unchanged by the forgetful functor.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 185

[Integrality descends to a retract of an integral scheme] Let \(X \times Y\) be an integral scheme (here it is geometrically irreducible and reduced over \(\bar k\)). Suppose \(X\) is a retract of \(X \times Y\): there is a section \(s \colon X \to X \times Y\) of the first projection \(p_1\), i.e. \(s \mathbin {\, ;\, }p_1 = \mathbf1_X\). Then \(X\) is integral.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 480An invertible module is locally trivial (locally free of rank \(1\))

Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(M \in \mathrm{Scheme}.\mathtt{Modules}\, X\). If \(M\) is \(\otimes \)-invertible (454), then \(M\) is locally trivial: every point of \(X\) has an open (affine) neighbourhood \(U\) on which \(M|_U \cong \mathcal{O}_U\). In symbols

\[ \mathtt{IsInvertible}(M) \; \Longrightarrow \; \mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\, M. \]

This implication is recorded for later use — the Quot-embedding of A.2.c — and is not on the path to 481. That corollary is obtained from the locally-trivial comparison 479, whose locally-trivial hypotheses are supplied directly by the consumer’s carrier (and, for the inverse object, by the easy reverse implication 421); this forward bridge is never invoked. Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemmas lemma-invertible and lemma-invertible-is-locally-free-rank-1: on a ringed space whose stalks are local rings, an invertible module is locally free of rank \(1\); and an invertible module is finitely presented (locally a direct summand of a finite free module).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 486

[The tensor inverse is determined up to isomorphism] Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR, Lemma lemma-invertible: when \(\mathcal{L}\) is invertible the module \(\mathcal{N}\) with \(\mathcal{L} \otimes _{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{N} \cong \mathcal{O}_X\) is isomorphic to the dual \(\mathcal{H}\! om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X)\), hence unique up to isomorphism. Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(M, N, N' \in \mathrm{Scheme}.\mathtt{Modules}\, X\). If \(M \otimes _X N \cong \mathcal{O}_X\) and \(M \otimes _X N' \cong \mathcal{O}_X\), then \(N \cong N'\). Consequently the assignment \([M] \mapsto [N]\), sending an invertible class to the class of its tensor inverse, is well-defined on \(\operatorname{Pic}X\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 481Pullback preserves \(\otimes \)-invertibility for a general scheme morphism

Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes and let \(M \in \mathrm{Scheme}.\mathtt{Modules}\, X\) be \(\otimes \)-invertible (454) with a tensor inverse \(N\) and isomorphism \(e : M \otimes _X N \cong \mathcal{O}_X\). If both \(M\) and \(N\) are locally trivial (383) — as holds for every line bundle, and in particular for the modules the relative Picard carrier presents — then the pullback \(f^*M\) is again \(\otimes \)-invertible, with inverse \(f^*N\):

\[ \mathtt{IsInvertible}(M) \; \Longrightarrow \; \mathtt{IsInvertible}\bigl((\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\, f).\mathrm{obj}\, M\bigr). \]

Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma lemma-pullback-invertible: the pullback \(f^*\mathcal{L}\) of an invertible \(\mathcal{O}\)-module along a morphism of ringed spaces is invertible.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 484

[\(\otimes \)-invertibility is closed under tensor product] Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR, Lemma lemma-constructions-invertible (item 1): the tensor product of two invertible \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is again invertible. Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(M, M' \in \mathrm{Scheme}.\mathtt{Modules}\, X\). If \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\, M\) and \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\, M'\), then \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M \otimes _X M')\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 485

[The structure sheaf is \(\otimes \)-invertible] Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR, Lemma lemma-invertible: an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module is invertible iff there exists \(\mathcal{N}\) with \(\mathcal{L} \otimes _{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{N} \cong \mathcal{O}_X\). Let \(X\) be a scheme. The structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\, X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\) is \(\otimes \)-invertible: \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\, \mathcal{O}_X\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 360Iso-reflection bridge for presheaf-of-modules morphisms

Let \(R \colon C^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathbf{RingCat}\) and let \(f \colon M \to N\) be a morphism of presheaves of modules over \(R\). If every component \(f.\mathrm{app}\, X\) is an isomorphism in \(\mathrm{ModuleCat}\, (R.\mathrm{obj}\, X)\), then \(f\) is an isomorphism. The bridge chains the iso-reflection of PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R with NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 505

[Local isomorphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules glue to a global isomorphism] Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\varphi \colon M \to N\) a morphism in \(\mathrm{Scheme}.\mathtt{Modules}\, X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\, X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\). Suppose that for every point \(x \in X\) there is an open neighbourhood \(U_x\) of \(x\) such that the restriction of \(\varphi \) to \(U_x\) — the image \((\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\, (U_x).\mathtt{i}).\mathtt{map}\, \varphi \) under the restriction functor along the open immersion \((U_x).\mathtt{i}\colon U_x \to X\) — is an isomorphism in \(\mathrm{Scheme}.\mathtt{Modules}\, U_x\). Then \(\varphi \) is an isomorphism.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 462

[Reduction of \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\) iso-ness to the sheafified presheaf \(\delta \)] Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes and let \(M, N \in \mathrm{Scheme}.\mathtt{Modules}\, X\). Write

\[ \varphi ' : (X.\mathtt{presheaf} \circ \mathtt{forget}_2) \; \longrightarrow \; (\mathtt{Opens.map}\, f.\mathtt{base})^{\mathrm{op}} \circ (Y.\mathtt{presheaf} \circ \mathtt{forget}_2) \]

for the induced presheaf-of-modules map presenting \(f\), and let \(a_Y = \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\, (\mathbf{1}_{Y.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}})\) denote sheafification on \(Y\). If the sheafified presheaf-level oplax comparison

\[ a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl( \delta \, (\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\, \varphi ')\, M.\mathtt{val}\, N.\mathtt{val} \bigr) \]

is an isomorphism, then the sheaf-level comparison \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\, f\, M\, N : f^*(M \otimes _X N) \to f^*M \otimes _Y f^*N\) of 461 is an isomorphism.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 413

[Sheafification inverts a localizer morphism of presheaves of modules] Let \(R_0\) be a presheaf of commutative rings on a site carrying a Grothendieck topology \(J\), let \(R_{sh}\) be its sheafification, and let \(\alpha : R_0 \to R_{sh}.\mathrm{obj}\) be the (locally bijective) comparison map of presheaves of rings. Let \(f : A \to B\) be a morphism of presheaves of \(R_0\)-modules whose underlying morphism of presheaves of abelian groups \((\mathtt{toPresheaf}\, R_0).\mathrm{map}\, f\) lies in the sheafification localizer class \(J.W\) — equivalently, is locally bijective for \(J\). Then the presheaf-of-modules sheafification functor \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\, \alpha \) sends \(f\) to an isomorphism:

\[ (\mathtt{sheafification}\, \alpha ).\mathrm{map}\, f \quad \text{is an isomorphism.} \]

This is a closed go/no-go bridge — it converts “ a morphism lies in \(J.W\) ” into “ its sheafification is invertible ”. Under route (e) the associator is the API-derived one (417), so this bridge is retained as a supplement rather than as the associator’s load-bearing step.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 471

[IsIso plumbing from the unit square] Suppose the unit square

\[ (\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\, f\, \mathcal{O}_X).\mathrm{inv} \; \mathbin {;}\; a_Y.\mathrm{map}\, (\eta \, F) \; \mathbin {;}\; \mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}.\mathrm{hom} \; =\; \mathtt{pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\, \varphi \]

commutes. Then \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}\, (\eta \, F)\) is an isomorphism.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 701Step B.b — affine-open stalk is a localisation

For an affine open \(V \ni z\) of a scheme \(X\), the stalk \(\mathcal O_{X,z}\) is the localisation of the section ring \(\Gamma (X, V)\) at the prime ideal of \(z\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 415

[Local injectivity of a whiskered localizer morphism (stalkwise, flatness-free)] Let \(R\) be a presheaf of commutative rings on a site carrying a Grothendieck topology \(J\), and let \(J.W\) be the sheafification localizer (the locally bijective morphisms). Concretely the site carries the \(\mathtt{WEqualsLocallyBijective}\) property for \(\mathtt{Ab}\), so \(J.W\) is exactly the class of \(J\)-locally bijective morphisms; this is recorded as a typeclass hypothesis \([J.\mathtt{WEqualsLocallyBijective}\, \mathtt{Ab}]\) on the statement. Let \(F\) be an arbitrary presheaf of \(R\)-modules, and let \(g : M \to N\) be a morphism of presheaves of \(R\)-modules whose underlying morphism of presheaves of abelian groups \((\mathtt{toPresheaf}\, R).\mathrm{map}\, g\) lies in \(J.W\). Then the left-whiskered morphism

\[ F \triangleleft g \; \; =\; \; \mathrm{id}_F \otimes g \]

is \(J\)-locally injective. No flatness and no local-triviality hypothesis on \(F\) is required.

This is the \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\)-field content (the injectivity half) of the full instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (417), the load-bearing field of the off-path \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) monoidal-category path (arbitrary \(F\)). Its content for an arbitrary \(F\) is exactly the live lemma 429, which the associator’s sheafification transport consumes and which is closed unconditionally by the d.2 stalk–tensor commutation (424); the d.1/d.2 ingredients are therefore on the critical path. The two proofs recorded below — a sheaf-level argument specialised to locally trivial \(F\), and the general stalkwise argument via (d.1)/(d.2) — are retained for the historical record; the live group law closes this obligation in its general (d.2) form through 429.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 429

[Unconditional left-whiskering of the localizer, via d.2] Let \(R\), \(J\) be as above with \(J.\mathtt{WEqualsLocallyBijective}\, \mathtt{Ab}\), let \(F\) be an arbitrary presheaf of \(R\)-modules, and let \(g : M \to N\) be a morphism whose underlying morphism lies in \(J.W\). Then the left-whiskered morphism \(F \triangleleft g = \mathrm{id}_F \otimes g\) is \(J\)-locally injective (indeed lies in \(J.W\)). No flatness and no local-triviality hypothesis on \(F\) is required. This discharges the open left-whiskering obligation 415 that the unconditional associator 418 consumes.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 384

[Pullback preserves local triviality (Stacks 01HH)] Source: Stacks 01HH (pullback of a locally trivial module is locally trivial). Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes and let \(\mathcal{M} : X.\mathrm{Modules}\) be locally trivial of rank one (383). Then \(f^*\mathcal{M}\) is locally trivial of rank one.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 770A scheme smooth over a field is reduced

For an object \(A\) over \(\operatorname{Spec}\bar k\) whose structure morphism \(A.\mathrm{hom}\) is smooth, the underlying scheme \(A.\mathrm{left}\) is reduced.

Proof ▶

Proved directly in Lean.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 705Step A1 bridge — ring-quotient cons rule for regular sequences

Let \(r \in A\) be such that multiplication by \(r\) is injective on \(A\), and suppose the images of a list \(rs\) form a regular sequence over the ring quotient \(A/(r)\). Then \(r :: rs\) is a regular sequence over \(A\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 742Regularity on a constant product

For a nonempty finite index type \(\iota \) and a list \(rs\) of elements of \(R\), the sequence \(rs\) is \((\iota \to M)\)-regular if and only if it is \(M\)-regular. The proof inducts on \(rs\): the empty case reduces to \(\text{Nontrivial}(\iota \to M) \leftrightarrow \text{Nontrivial}(M)\), and the cons case combines pointwise \(\text{SMul}\)-regularity with 741 to feed the inductive hypothesis.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 782Geometric irreducibility of \(\operatorname{Pic}^0_{C/k}\)

The Jacobian scheme \(J = \operatorname{Pic}^0_{C/k}\) (778) is geometrically irreducible over \(\bar k\), read off from the corresponding field of its Pic\(^0\) bundle.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 779Group-object structure on \(\operatorname{Pic}^0_{C/k}\)

The Jacobian scheme \(J = \operatorname{Pic}^0_{C/k}\) (778) carries a group-object structure, read off from the corresponding field of its Pic\(^0\) bundle.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 780Properness of \(\operatorname{Pic}^0_{C/k}\)

The Jacobian scheme \(J = \operatorname{Pic}^0_{C/k}\) (778) is proper over \(\bar k\), read off from the corresponding field of its Pic\(^0\) bundle.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 781Smoothness of \(\operatorname{Pic}^0_{C/k}\)

The Jacobian scheme \(J = \operatorname{Pic}^0_{C/k}\) (778) is smooth over \(\bar k\), read off from the corresponding field of its Pic\(^0\) bundle.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 417The \(J.W\)-monoidality instance and the localized monoidal substrate (route (e) target)

Let \(X\) be a scheme. The sheafification localizer \(J.W\) on \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\, \mathcal{O}_X\) is monoidal: it carries an instance

\[ J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}, \]

whose two fields are the whiskering-stability lemmas 416 (and which is multiplicative because sheafification inverts identities and composites). Consequently, by Mathlib’s \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) (20.2) applied to the already-monoidal category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\, \mathcal{O}_X\), the localization functor \(L = \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\) (which is the localization at \(J.W\)), and the unit isomorphism \(L(\mathbf{1}) \cong \mathcal{O}_X\), the category

\[ \mathrm{Scheme}.\mathtt{Modules}\, X \; =\; \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\, X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf} \]

acquires a full \(\mathrm{MonoidalCategory}\) structure whose tensor is the substrate \(\otimes _X\) (403) and whose associator, left/right unitors, braiding and all coherence (pentagon, triangle, naturalities) are derived by the API. This is the route (e) target: the associator 418, the unitors 419 and the braiding 420 are then components of this derived monoidal structure rather than hand-assembled composites.

This block states the route (e) construction; it depends on the sole open obligation 415. It mirrors Mathlib’s \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\) construction one categorical level up.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 165

[Kähler module of a localization is trivial] Let \(A \to L\) be a localization at a multiplicative subset \(M \subseteq A\). Then \(\Omega _{L / A}\) is subsingleton (the zero \(L\)-module).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 166

[Tower-cancellation equivalence for Kähler modules under localization]

Let \(A \to L\) be a localization (in the sense of IsLocalization), and let \(L \to B\) be a ring map forming a scalar tower \(A \to L \to B\). Then the canonical \(B\)-linear map \(\Omega _{B/A} \to \Omega _{B/L}\) induced by the tower is a \(B\)-linear equivalence.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 352(ii-\(\beta \)-aux-chart) Image of the base field is integrally closed in a chart of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible scheme — off-path record (iter-155)
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 355

[(ii-\(\beta \)-core ring-side) Kernel of the universal Kähler derivation: \(D b = 0\) implies \(b\) in the image of the base field] Let \(k\) be an algebraically closed field of characteristic zero ([Field k] [IsAlgClosed k] [CharZero k]), and let \(B\) be a finite-type standard-smooth \(k\)-algebra of relative dimension \(n\) that is moreover an integral domain ([Algebra.FiniteType k B], [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k B], [IsDomain B]).
